#### 2.4.2.1.0-2 Power Saving Bandwidth Part Switch Control Overview of Power Saving Bandwidth Part switch state machine (ID: 11153110)

**2.4.2.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11153115`)

Power Saving Bandwidth Part Switch Control When a UE is configured with at least one power saving bandwidth part (PS BWP), L2-PS shall control the bandwidth part switching (between Power saving BWP and larger BWP) dynamically using DCI 1_1. To begin UE always starts from larger BWP and algorithm to monitor the BWP switch activity shall get triggered upon receiving PS BWP usage information from CP-RT during PsUser_UserModifyReq + PsUser_RrcReconfCompletedReq OR PsUser_UserSetupReq as explained in 5G_L2_9844_replaced([11153158](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24sQm_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

UE state transitions within Large and Power saving BWP is as follows

#### 2.4.2.1.1 UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch (ID: 11153152)

#### 2.4.2.1.1-1 UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch UE State transition at UL Scheduler for BWP Switch (ID: 11153238)

**2.4.2.1.1-1.0-3**  (ID: `11153258`)

UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch 1. BWP default state (No BWP Evaluation) : This is the default state. Same conditions as per DL Scheduler apply to this state handling also.

2. Evaluation state (Evaluation for BWP Switch) : This is the state which maintains the UE in normal scheduling conditions in a given BWP. BWP switch evaluation happens at DL Scheduler and UE maintains this state until there is a trigger from DL Scheduler to free the UL HARQ transmissions using DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate.BWPSwitchUpdate with request Type as "Free HARQ and Stop Scheduling". Such request shall change the UE to next state " Waiting for UL HARQ Free "

1. Waiting for UL HARQ Free : Moved to this state if UE is found eligible for BWP switch and UL Scheduler has received DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate.BWPSwitchUpdate with request Type as "Free HARQ and Stop Scheduling". Here in this state the UE is blocked for new UL HARQ transmissions and checked for any pending UL HARQ retransmissions. A maximum of 64 slots are considered as wait period to clear all pending UL HARQ retransmissions. Beyond this wait period, the UE frees pending UL HARQ IDs. Once all the UL HARQ pending retransmissions are cleared, the UL Scheduler shall send UlToDlIntraSchedUpdate.BWPSwitchHARQFree by adding this CRNTI and change the state to "Waiting for completion".

2. Waiting for completion : UL scheduler waits in this state until it receives DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate.BWPSwitchUpdate with request Type as "Resume Scheduling in Large BWP" or "Resume Scheduling in PS BWP". Upon such message, its confirmed that the UE has now moved to the Target BWP and UL scheduler can resume new transmissions getting scheduled in same way as done in DL after updating UE context with new BWP ID. Now the UE is moved to evaluation state.

Steps 5 and 6 are applicable only if NRBTS/NRCELL.actInactiveBwpScanning is set to TRUE (When BWP scanning is enabled to help UE recover from possible BWP mismatch situation)

1. UL Sched EO monitors UE’s response to grants issued for SR requests. When 5 such consecutive UL grants are DTXed, it implies the UE could may not be in the BWP what gNB assumes it to be. At this stage, UE state is moved to BWP recovery state UL Sched EO sends the message stopSchedulingForBwpRematch to DL Sched EO and UE’s BWP is updated with the target BWP. gNB issues UL DCI 0_1 to monitor PUSCH over this new target BWP (gNB assumed inactive BWP).

2. Upon receiving 5 non DTX responses from UE towards the UL grants issued in response to SR, UL Sched EO assumes the UE to be in target BWP. This step implies the gNB and the UE are now aligned with respect the UE’s BWP. UL Sched EO sends the message resumeSchedulingOnNewBwp to DL Sched EO and UE is moved out of BWP recovery state.

[03147365]

When BWP scanning is enabled(NRBTS/NRCELL.actInactiveBwpScanning is set to TRUE) and UE is under BWP scanning procedure, if L2-PS recevies MAC control element , C-RNTI for the UE. Refer 5G_UP_ALG_16104_replaced([11111313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpMPyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

UL sched EO sends message resumeSchedulingOnActiveBwp to DL Sched EO. This indicates reception of MAC control element, C-RNTI for UE and L2PS has to roll back to source BWP(at start of BWP scanning) and stop/abort BWP scanning procedure.

[End 03147365]

---

##### 2.4.2.1.1.0-1 UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch Default state (ID: 11153158)

**2.4.2.1.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11153169`)

UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch 1. BWP default state (No BWP Evaluation) : This is the default state. The UE stays in this state till the time CP-RT indicates the usage of Power Saving Bandwidth Part (PS BWP) using bwpConfig (5G_L2_IF_408_replaced([10975247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_3Dfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) within PsUser_UserModifyReq + PsUser_RrcReconfCompletedReq OR PsUser_UserSetupReq as explained below.

(i) In case of Initial Context Setup (ICS) and Intra DU handover cases, L2-PS receives PsUser_UserModifyReq containing bwpConfig to indicate PS BWP usage or ' 2 bit ' BWP indicator usage in the DCI for the UE. Moves to the next state "Evaluation for BWP Switch" after the reception of PsUser_RrcReconfCompletedReq to indicate the RRC Reconfiguration Complete from the UE side.

(ii) In case of Inter DU or Inter gNB handover cases the L2 PS receives PsUser_UserSetupReq containing bwpConfig to indicate PS BWP usage or ' 2 bit ' BWP indicator usage in the DCI for the UE. Moves to the next state "Evaluation for BWP Switch" [Before 03573400] immediately [03573400] once the RACH procedure is completed. [End 03573400].

---

##### 2.4.2.1.1.0-2 UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch Evaluation state (ID: 11153176)

**2.4.2.1.1.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11153202`)

UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch 2. Evaluation state (Evaluation for BWP Switch) : This is the state which evaluates the timing and other conditions for the BWP switch to be triggered in either of the directions(Larger to PS BWP OR PS to Larger BWP). First time initiation of this state will be with the UE in larger BWP and L2-PS shall start UE level timer with value measurementDurationToPsBwp. Next iterations are those instances when the UE completes the BWP switch and enters Evaluation state. Here the timer shall be restarted with value equal to measurementDurationFromPsBwp when the UE enters PS BWP OR measurementDurationToPsBwp when the UE enters larger BWP. Evaluation is performed using conditions mentioned in 5G_UP_ALG_1870_replaced([11111163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when UE is in PS BWP and 5G_UP_ALG_1872_replaced([11111200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when UE is in large BWP. Evaluation criteria decides if the UE requires a BWP switch.

Along with the evalution criteria mentioned in 5G_UP_ALG_1870_replaced([11111163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBlPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(when UE is in PS BWP) and 5G_UP_ALG_1872_replaced([11111200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))(when UE is in large BWP), an additional check is applied such that

- UE shall not be in RRC Reconfiguration Transient phase and should have completed the RRC Reconfguration procedure, to be considered eligeable to preform BWP switch.

 If the UE is within the large BWP, and it fulfills the evaluation criteria as per conditions mentioned in 5G_UP_ALG_1872_replaced([11111200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), an additional check is applied such that

If ( UE is configured with at least one VoNR flow )

If ( ( Existing number VoNR UEs in PS BWP + *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving ) < *maxNumberVoNRPowerSaving )**

&& ( Existing number UEs in PS BWP + *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving ) < *maxNumberUesPowerSaving ) )**

Evaluation for moving to PS BWP is successful

Else

Evaluation for moving to PS BWP has failed. UE to continue in large BWP with measurements for the next cycle.

Else

If ( Existing number UEs in PS BWP + *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving ) < *maxNumberUesPowerSaving**

Evaluation for moving to PS BWP is successful

Else

Evaluation for moving to PS BWP has failed. UE to continue in large BWP with measurements for the next cycle.

If evaluation criteria are successful either for large to PS BWP or PS to large BWP switch, below steps (i) to (iv) are followed

(i) If the UE is moving from large to PS BWP, increment the count *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving **and increment the count *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving if the UE is configured with at least one VoNR flow****

(ii) Change state to "Waiting for UL and DL HARQ Free" if all DL HARQ processes are not free OR change state to "Waiting for UL HARQ Free" if all DL HARQ processes are free.

(iii) Include this UE in to inform UL scheduler to free up all HARQ Processes for this CRNTI (by sending request type as "Free HARQ and Stop Scheduling" for particular CRNTI). UL SCH to reply back in by adding this particular CRNTI once all UL HARQ are free. UL scheduler to stop scheduling for this UE after freeing up all HARQ processes.

(iv) Stop UE level evaluation timer

**[CB007880-C]**

If UE is already in PS BWP which is smaller than C_srs for SRS positioning (5G_UP_ALG_27064_replaced([11107865](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kojWmvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) when precise positioning is to be configured,5G-L2-PS shall switch the UE's BWP to the dedicated BWP that is higher than C_srs when UL BWP switch from power saving BWP.

**[End CB007880-C]**

[CNI-130786]

If evaluation criteria are successful either for large to PS BWP or PS to large BWP switch as [11153131](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/web#action=com.ibm.rdm.web.pages.showArtifactPage&artifactURI=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Frm%2Fresources%2FBI_24sQmPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ&oslc.configuration=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), as stated in[12702850](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_BX8XQF44EfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), 5G-L2-PS shall intentionally add extra bits to the FDRA size of the M first repetitions of BWP switch DCI (where M is rdTestModeBwpSwitchFailureRepetitions: [12702888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_rIbgwF46EfCLrcnPCspVyQ)).
 Note, maximum repetitions of BWP switch DCI is capped by rdNumberOfBwpSwitchRepetitions. Here for CNI, it needs to malform FDRA size upto rdTestModeBwpSwitchFailureRepetitions times.
 Expectation, if rdTestModeBwpSwitchFailureRepetitions < rdNumberOfBwpSwitchRepetitions , then bwp switch should be successful.
 If rdTestModeBwpSwitchFailureRepetitions >= rdNumberOfBwpSwitchRepetitions, then BWP switch should fail as depicted in CNI document. And bwp scanning and bwp recovery procedure should follow.

About extra bits to the FDRA size, add three extra bytes in calculate FDRA size. Expectation UE will not be able to decode DCI FDRA size is malfomed.

[End CNI-130786]

Note :

1. *rdSwitchTimertoPsBwp and *rdSwitchTimerFromPsBwp are used to maintain UEs for minimum durations within larger and PS BWP respectively. This period is also referred to as hysteresis duration in the given BWP.**

2. *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving depicts the number of UEs which are currently moving from Larger to PS BWP after their evaluation criteria was fulfilled and not yet switched into PS BWP. Such UEs are tracked to allow their BWP transition during HARQ freeing and other states involved before they finally enter PS BWP.*

3. The 5QI 1/5QI**-65/5QI-66 enables the support of IMS conversational voice services on NR. Hence 5QI 15QI**-65/5QI-66 **is referred to as VoNR flow and UEs configured with at least one VoNR flow are considered as VoNR UEs.******

4. 5G_UP_ALG_1872_replaced([11111200](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpLBnfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) limits the number of VoNR UEs (UEs with at least one VoNR flow) entering PS BWP upto maxNumberVoNRPowerSaving. Inline with this condition, UEs during their transition into PS BWP need to be differentiated based on thier VoNR and non-VoNR criteria. *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving count considers combined total number of VoNR and Non-VoNR UEs. *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving depicts the number of UEs which are configured with at least one VoNR flow and are currently moving from Larger to PS BWP after their evaluation criteria was fulfilled and not yet switched into PS BWP.**

5.When UE is in Large BWP and has any SCell active then evaluation shall not started if possible or fail. Evaluation shall be failed at the earliest possible phase. UE is treated as not requiring BWP switch, hence restart of evaluation time shall be done.

1. This Requirement Id of BWP switching is applicable between PS and any of the dedicated BWP which can be either Large or Mid or Small BWP.

---

##### 2.4.2.1.1.0-3 UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch Other states (ID: 11153207)

**2.4.2.1.1.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `13455498`)

UE State transition at DL Scheduler for BWP Switch 3. Waiting for UL and DL HARQ Free : Moved to this state if the UE is found eligible for BWP switch with both UL and DL HARQ pending retransmissions. After all the pending DL Retransmissions are cleared, the UE moves to the next state "Waiting for UL HARQ Free".

1. Waiting for UL HARQ Free : Moved to this state if UE is found eligible for BWP switch with only UL HARQ pending retransmissions.

2. Waiting for DL HARQ Free : Moved to this state if UE is found eligible for BWP switch with only DL HARQ pending retransmissions. A maximum of 64 slots are considered as wait period to clear all pending DL HARQ retransmissions. Beyond this wait period, the UE frees pending DL HARQ IDs and moves to the next state 'Ready for DCI Tx'

Note : In all the states when UE is waiting to clear the HARQ retransmissions (DL / UL / Both DL and UL) addition of UE in CS1 list to be skipped if UE's candidate List Priority index maps to NewTx. This allows UE to block scheduling any new transmissions and to continue only pending retransmissions.

1. Ready for DCI Tx : Moved to this state if the UE has no pending retransmissions for both UL and DL HARQs. When UE gets added in CS1 list from the candidate list, the TD scheduling checks the UE state to be "Ready for DCI Tx" and UE becomes eligible to get a DCI for BWP Switch. If BWP Switch DCI is scheduled, TD scheduler will change the Ue state to "Waiting for BWP Switch Completion" and will inform pre scheduler with "Scheduling Done" event adds an entry in updateList which leads to adding the UE in priority list post current scheduling.

2. Waiting for BWP Switch Completion(N + K0_PS_BWP + K1 period) : This state indicates that the UE has been scheduled a DCI 1_1 in order to perform the BWP switch and gNB is awaiting a response from the UE to confirm the switch completion. During this period, UE remains in the candidate list and not added in the CS1 List for traffic scheduling. A maximum of 64 slots are considered as wait period to clear pending DL HARQ retransmission for the DCI switch transmission. Beyond this wait period, the UE frees pending HARQ IDs and moves to the previous state 'Ready for DCI Tx'.

Upon receiving HARQ feedback during the current state,

(i) If DTX or ACK received, change state to "Ready for DCI Tx". As normal DTX process, UE shall be scheduled another DCI attempting the BWP switch again. If this continues multiple times and UE does not switch the BWP, eventually RLF gets triggered after continuous DTX (5G_UP_ALG_3350_replaced([11151610](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksai1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_L2_IF_530_replaced([10977521](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VNSY_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In order to avoid useless interaction with ReTx L2Ps internal mechanism all retransmitted DCI for BWP switch will be set as fresh Tx with new harqId

(ii) If NACK received, it indicates successful BWP switch and UE acknowledging the DCI switch information it received earlier. L2 PS shall perform the steps (a) to (e)

(a) If the UE has moved from Large to PS BWP, decrement the count *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving and decrement the count *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving if the UE is configured with at least one VoNR flow**

(b) If the UE has moved from Large to PS BWP, increment the count for number active UEs in PS BWP

(c) Update UE context to new BWP retaining the UE in the candidate list

(d) Indicate UL scheduler to resume scheduling on the new BWP by sending DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate.BWPSwitchUpdate request type as "Resume Scheduling in BWP Large BWP" or "Resume Scheduling in BWP PS BWP" containing the UE CRNTI

(e) Move the UE to "Evaluation state"

Steps 8 and 9 are applicable only if NRBTS/NRCELL.actInactiveBwpScanning is set to TRUE (When BWP scanning is enabled to help UE recover from possible BWP mismatch situation)

1. DL Sched EO receives the message stopSchedulingForBwpRematch from UL Sched EO. This indicates UE has failed to use(DTXed) 5 consecutive UL grants issued in response to SRs. Upon message reception, DL Sched moves the UE to BWP recovery state and suspends DL scheduling.

2. DL Sched EO receives the message resumeSchedulingOnNewBwp from UL Sched EO. This indicates UE has responded after the BWP switch was done internally at the gNB and PUSCH grants on the updated BWP were non DTX. This step implies the gNB and the UE are now aligned with respect the UE’s BWP. UE is now moved out of BWP recovery state, DL scheduling is resumed and UE’s BWP is updated with the target BWP.

When BWP scanning is enabled(NRBTS/NRCELL.actInactiveBwpScanning is set to TRUE) and UE is under BWP scanning procedure, if L2-PS recevies MAC control element , C-RNTI for the UE. Refer 5G_UP_ALG_16104_replaced([11111313](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpMPyvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DL sched EO receives the message resumeSchedulingOnActiveBwp from UL Sched EO. This indicates reception of MAC control element, C-RNTI for UE and L2PS has to roll back to source BWP(at start of BWP scanning) and stop/abort BWP scanning procedure.

Note :

1. If the UE was moving from Large to PS BWP and was in any state other than "Evaluation state", and the UE context is deleted due to RLF or any scenario triggering PsUser_UserDeleteReq(5G_L2_IF_5365_replaced([10976800](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VIZ7Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), L2 PS decrements the count *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving and decrements the count *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving if the UE is configured with at least one VoNR flow during the user deletion procedure.**

2. VoNR flow deletion during PS BWP transition : If the UE is already considered in the count of *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving and the last VoNR flow is removed, decrement the count *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving**

3. VoNR flow addition during PS BWP transition : If the UE is already considered in the count of *TotalTransitUesPowerSaving and is not considered in the count *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving and it is added with the first VoNR flow, increment the count *TotalTransitVoNRUesPowerSaving***

4. If BWP scanning is enabled (NRBTS/NRCELL.actInactiveBwpScanning is set to TRUE), then for UEs that have triggered by L2-PS the BWP scanning procedure, the PS BWP counters are pegged only when the BWP scanning procedure successfully completes. The following AVG_UE_ACTIVE_PWR_SAVING_BWP and MAX_UE_ACTIVE_PWR_SAVING_BWP are derived from PS BWP counter

5. K0_PS_BWP = 6 except for the frame structure of tdLteConfig0, for tdLteConfig0 K0_PS_BWP is 8, refer to [11111082: DCI-based BWP switch procedure](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9).

[05568582] 6. When PDCCH muting is triggered (see [13242857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2uvgIMKhEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ) and [13242859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_jt_WkMKiEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ)), the DCI 1_1, where k0 is equal to 6 may be scheduled on muted symbols ([11360543](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucvIOfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [End 05568582]

#### 2.4.2.1.2 PM counter update for PS BWP (ID: 11153262)
##### 2.4.2.1.2.0-1 PM counter update for PS BWP (ID: 11153269)

**2.4.2.1.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11153277`)

PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP 11153277: PM counter update for PS BWP Below mentioned PM counters shall be updated by L2-PS 1. MAX_UE_ACTIVE_PWR_SAVING_BWP (M55351C00001) : L2 PS shall report to PM agent, the maximum number of active UEs in PS BWP every sampling interval. Each sampling interval can elapse across several slots. L2 PS computes maximum number of active UEs in PS BWP between each slot and shall report to PM-Agent once in every sampling interval. Note: PM agent further reports the maximum number of active UEs in PS BWP to OAM every measurement period (could be several sampling intervals) 2. AVG_UE_ACTIVE_PWR_SAVING_BWP (M55351C00002) : L2 PS shall report to PM agent, the average number of active UEs in PS BWP every sampling interval. Each sampling interval can elapse across several slots. L2 PS computes average number of active UEs in PS BWP between each slot and shall report to PM-Agent once in every sampling interval. During the computation if the average value results in a fraction, it will be either Ceiled Or Floored to its nearest value. The example illustrates the L2-PS behaviour for counter updates. The example uses some potential values for number of active UEs in PS BWP during each slot and illustrates how L2-PS uses those to compute max and average values it reports to PM agent. Sampling interval time duration : 1 second Number of slots in 1 sampling interval : 2000

Could not load image (check sign-in and GCS access).

L2 PS reports MAX_UE_ACTIVE_PWR_SAVING_BWP to PM agent for (i) Sampling interval 1 = 7 (ii) Sampling interval 2 = 3 L2 PS reports AVG_UE_ACTIVE_PWR_SAVING_BWP to PM agent for (i) Sampling interval 1 = (2 + 4 + 5 + 4 + 6 + 7 ... + 3) / 2000 (ii) Sampling interval 2 = (0 + 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 + 3 ... + 2) / 2000 File name : PS_BWP_PM_Counter_Illustration.vsdx Picture can be found here : Note:

1. PM agent further reports the maximum and average number of active UEs in PS BWP to OAM every measurement period (could be several sampling intervals).
2. PM agent computes the average using the formula Average = Value / Weight where, Value = Sum of average values received from L2 PS for 'n' sampling intervals Weight = Total number of sampling intervals, which is 'n'.
3. L2 PS does not provide weight values to PM agent explicitly. PM agent applies a weight of '1' for every sample it receives during a sampling interval.</p>

### 2.4.2.2 UE and L2-PS Active BWP (ID: 11153284)
#### 2.4.2.2.0-1 UE and L2-PS Active BWP **Active BWP Modification** (ID: 13470131)

**2.4.2.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13470133`)

UE and L2-PS Active BWP Modification of UE active BWP can be triggered by C-Plane, 5G-L2-PS or by User Equipment (UE) itself according to [11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

Not every modification of the UE’s active Bandwidth Part (BWP) leads to an RRC Reconfiguration. The system uses different mechanisms depending on the nature of the change. Lightweight, dynamic changes between pre-configured dedicated BWPs are handled by DCI (see [11111082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), while more fundamental changes to a BWP's parameters or recovery from certain states require a full RRC Reconfiguration.

The modification of an active Bandwidth Part (BWP) is a comprehensive process that extends far beyond just the "core" parameters of bandwidth and frequency location which are defined in bwpConfig structure (see [10975247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_3Dfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) in PsUser_UserModifyReq.cellConfigurationForModification structure (see [10976438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkufu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))

A BWP's configuration defines a complete operational environment for the UE, and this includes a wide range of UE-specific parameters for physical channels and procedures such as:

• PDSCH: Configuration from pdschConfig, including mcsTable and dmrsType.

• PUSCH: Configuration from pusch-Config, including PUSCH region definition (longPucchStartPuschPrb, longPucchNumPuschPrb) and mcsTable (see [10976438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkufu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• PUCCH: UE-specific configuration from pucch-Config (see [10976438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkufu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• PRACH: Configuration based on the presence of the prachConfigNotPresent parameter (see [10975247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_3Dfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• SRS: Configuration from srsConfig, isTasSrsForSubbandBmConfigured and isPeriodDoubledForSrs (see [10975247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U_3Dfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• Configured Grant: Configuration ulCgConfigType2 from PsUser_UserModifyReq message (see [10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), if the feature is active (NRCELL/actUlCgType2 is TRUE).

• Scheduling Request: Configuration from the perSrConfig[] array (see [10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• Measurement Gap: Configuration from the measGapConfig structure (see [10975833](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VC6Wfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• DRX: Configuration from the drxConfig structure (see [10975509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBFLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

• CSI-RS: Configuration from perCsiReportConfig, perCsiRsResConfig, and actAperCsi within the cellConfigurationForModification structure (see [10976438](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VGkufu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

#### 2.4.2.2.0-2 UE and L2-PS Active BWP Active BWP during the RA procedure (ID: 11153291)

**2.4.2.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11153298`)

UE and L2-PS Active BWP Informative: see Active BWP management during the Random Access procedure in 5G_UP_ALG_1862_replaced([11110853](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-SvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.2.2.0-3 UE and L2-PS Active BWP Active BWP update with the CBRA RA-Msg3 information (ID: 11153304)

**2.4.2.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11153310`)

UE and L2-PS Active BWP * Upon reception of LoCtrl_UlMacPduReceiveInd (with isUlCcchData = true, UL CCCH message containing RA Msg3) form L2-LO, L2-PS shall update UE context with active BWP set to initial BWP (BWP-0).

In that case, C-Plane will follow up with setting up remaining part of UE context (PsUser_UserSetupReq, 5G_L2_122_replaced([11143796](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_232jEvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), alongside with UE's configured dedicated BWP. L2-PS shall compare this BWP with initial BWP set as the above on the CCCH data reception, and check if there is the need to perform BWP switch and which resources are available for UE scheduling after the RA procedure is completed (5G_UP_ALG_17939_replaced([11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Rationale: Unlike the CFRA procedure where the UE and its active BWP are known at the RA-Msg1 reception, the UE active BWP for CBRA procedure shall be determined as above.

- Upon the reception of LoCtrl_UlMacPduReceiveInd (with isMacCrntiCe = true) from L2-LO, L2-PS recognizes that uplink C-RNTI MAC Control Element was received (the UE is RRC connected), and checks in the UE context associated with RNTI received, which BWP is active as follows:

- The UE active BWP context is not the BWP-0 and it is linked to a BWP without RACH configuration. This means that the UE triggered an autonomous BWP switch to the initial BWP and the L2-PS shall update the current UE active BWP to BWP-0 in the UE context.

- The UE active BWP context is not the BWP-0 and it is linked to a BWP configured with RACH. The L2-PS shall assume that the valid UE active BWP is already defined in the UE context.
- The UE active BWP context corresponds to the BWP-0. The L2-PS considers that the current UE active BWP is the BWP-0.

 In that case, the C-Plane already created the UE context in the U-Plane before the reception of the RA-Msg3 (via PsUser_UserSetupReq, 5G_L2_122_replaced([11143796](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_232jEvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). In this state, L2-PS shall maintain the current UE active BWP and will manage the modification(s) if needed (5G_L2_11487_replaced([11153317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24tetvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_17939_replaced([11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

The remaining actions triggered by the L2-PS on the reception of MAC CE CRNTI inside a RA Msg3 are described in the requirement 5G_UP_ALG_3436_replaced([11152921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksjFsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

CP2 reference: 5G_UP_ALG_17933_replaced([11110870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpH-TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.2.2.0-4 UE and L2-PS Active BWP Active BWP after the RA completion with or without DL-CCCH or DL-DCCH transmission (ID: 11153317)

**2.4.2.2.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11153324`)

UE and L2-PS Active BWP The L2-PS shall monitor and maintain the current UE active BWP until the UE context is released, as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_17939_replaced([11110885](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpIlUfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

### 2.4.2.3 Initial Bandwidth Part (ID: 11153330)
#### 2.4.2.3.0-1 Initial Bandwidth Part Initial Bandwidth Part - general information (ID: 11153336)

#### 2.4.3.2.0-2 Configuration Beamforming Behavior (ID: 11153463)

**2.4.3.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11153475`)

Configuration There is different spatial operating mode of a cell, please see 5G_UP_910_replaced([11353407](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ub_hU_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more detail. 5G-L2-PS shall decide how to control the behavior of beamforming for different spatial operating mode during cell setup:

• Non-Beamforming (Non-BF): Supported for FR1 FDD and FR1 TDD

If (*actBeamforming = False) and (*numOfSubSectors = 0) and *numOfSuperSubcells = 0, then cell is in Non-Beamforming mode:***

• Beamforming is disabled.

• Single SSB is used.

• Analog Beamforming (Analog-BF): Supported for FR2 TDD

If (*rrmBeamformingType = *analogBf), then cell is in Analog Beamforming mode:**

• Beamforming is enabled, Analog Beamforming is used.

• One or more SSB transmission is used.

• Only beamforming with SSB Beam is applied.

• If actBeamforming = False, beamforming with single sector beam is applied.

• Digital Beamforming (Digital-BF): Supported for FR1 TDD

If (*rrmBeamformingType = digitalBf) and (*actBeamforming = True) and (*numOfSubSectors = 0), then cell is in Digital Beamforming mode***

• Beamforming is enabled, Digital Beamforming is used.

• One or more SSB transmission is used.

• More type of beams could be used depends on various beamforming related feature flags, like: SSB Beams, Refine beams, PMI beams, SRS based 2D-DFT beams, etc.

NOTE: There is still one special case for FR1 TDD, if *actBeamforming = False, but RU is configured with basic beam set #1 (*bfSetup.beamSet = BEAMSET_1), single SSB transmission applied by beamforming with sector beam. [CB014301-B] In case of basicBeamSet can be online modifeid, and then L2-PS shall receive PsCell_BeamConfigUpdateReq from CP-RT ([12536044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_wLou8R9PEfCQJ5daVHVkgg)).[End CB014301-B]**

• Subsectorization: Supported for FR1 TDD

If (*actBeamforming = True) and (*numOfSubSectors = 2 or 4), then cell is in Subsectorization mode:**

• Digital Beamforming is used.

• Only beamforming with SSB Beam is applied.

• Single SSB beam per each sub-sector formed by beamforming.

• Super cell: Supported for FR1 TDD

If (*actBeamforming = False) and *numOfSuperSubcells is in {2,3,4,5,6} **and (*numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks is in {2,3,4,5,6}), then cell is Super cell Mode:*****

• Beamforming is not really applied.

• Multiple SSB transmission is used.

• Each subcell has its own SSB with unique index.

NOTE: *numOfSuperSubcells value equal to 0 (see 5G_L2_IF_73_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicates supercell is disabled i.e. NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/actSuperCell is set to false, otherwise supercell is enabled.*

**[CB009543-A]**

• Super cell: Supported for FR1 FDD

If (*actBeamforming = False) and *numOfSuperSubcells is in {2,3,4,5,6} **then cell is Super cell Mode:****

• Beamforming is not applied.

• All subcells have the same SSB (1 SSB for supercell).

NOTE: *numOfSuperSubcells value equal to 0 (see 5G_L2_IF_73_replaced([10967202](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) indicates supercell is disabled i.e. NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/actSuperCell is set to false, otherwise supercell is enabled.*

**[End CB009543-A]**

---

#### 2.4.3.2.0-3 Configuration Relation of csirsPatternId and antenna ports (informativ) (ID: 11153481)

**2.4.3.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11153486`)

Configuration Following information/notes are copied from description field of IE startCsirsPmiPatternId of PsCell_CellSetupReq to here.

Note: 4 patternIDs are needed for one CSI-RS resource, and each patternID serves the scheduling of 4 CSI-RS antenna ports on a certain OFDM symbol:

- csirsPatternId[0] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6} on symbol 0

- csirsPatternId[1] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7} on symbol 0

- csirsPatternId[2] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6} on symbol 1

- csirsPatternId[3] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7} on symbol 1

see 5G_L1_5G21A_832 for some detail about this mapping.

When 32 port CSI RS enable (act32PortCsiRs is True), 16 patternIDs(from startCsirsPmiPatternId to startCsirsPmiPatternId+15) are used for one 32 port CSI-RS resource.

- csirsPatternId[0] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6,p16,p18,p20,p22} on symbol 0

- csirsPatternId[1] -> {p8,p10,p12,p14,p24,p26,p28,p30} on symbol 0

- csirsPatternId[2] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7,p17,p19,p21,p23} on symbol 0

- csirsPatternId[3] -> {p9,p11,p13,p15,p25,p27,p29,p31} on symbol 0

- csirsPatternId[4] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6,p16,p18,p20,p22} on symbol 1

- csirsPatternId[5] -> {p8,p10,p12,p14,p24,p26,p28,p30} on symbol 1

- csirsPatternId[6] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7,p17,p19,p21,p23} on symbol 1

- csirsPatternId[7] -> {p9,p11,p13,p15,p25,p27,p29,p31} on symbol 1

- csirsPatternId[8] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6,p16,p18,p20,p22} on symbol 2

- csirsPatternId[9] -> {p8,p10,p12,p14,p24,p26,p28,p30} on symbol 2

- csirsPatternId[10] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7,p17,p19,p21,p23} on symbol 2

- csirsPatternId[11] -> {p9,p11,p13,p15,p25,p27,p29,p31} on symbol 2

- csirsPatternId[12] -> {p0,p2,p4,p6,p16,p18,p20,p22} on symbol 3

- csirsPatternId[13] -> {p8,p10,p12,p14,p24,p26,p28,p30} on symbol 3

- csirsPatternId[14] -> {p1,p3,p5,p7,p17,p19,p21,p23} on symbol 3

- csirsPatternId[15] -> {p9,p11,p13,p15,p25,p27,p29,p31} on symbol 3

Note, csirsPatternId[x] is:

csirsPatternId[0] -> startCsirsPmiPatternId

csirsPatternId[1] -> startCsirsPmiPatternId+1

...

csirsPatternId[15] -> startCsirsPmiPatternId+15

### 2.4.3.3 Beam Management (ID: 11153492)
#### 2.4.3.3.1 Initial beam selection (ID: 11153498)
##### 2.4.3.3.1.0-1 Initial beam selection Initial beam selection from PRACH (ID: 11153505)

**2.4.3.3.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `11153532`)

Initial beam selection 5G-L2-PS receives cell level rach configuration parameters in PSCell_CellSetupReq(5G_L2_IF_61_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) message:

prachConfigurationIndex

nbrOfSsbPerRachOccasion

msg1Fdm

numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks

5G-L2-PS receives preamble dedicated parameters in UlData_PrachReceiveInd (refer to 5G_L1_IF_234_replaced([11514309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_FR2oWvvxEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

sfn

slot

prachPreambleTimeOccasion

prachPreambleFreqOccasion

Algorithm:

 Calculate NumberOfPrachOccasionsPerSlot based on prachConfigurationIndex parameter

Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_1959_replaced([11111903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRIOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Calculate NumberOfPrachSlotsInAssociationPeriod

Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_1962_replaced([11111925](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRIPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Calculate rrmPrachSlotIndex:

Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_1965_replaced([11111936](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Calculate rrmPrachOccasionIndex:

Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_1965_replaced([11111936](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 Calcalute associated SSB

Refer to 5G_UP_1970_replaced([11377269](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueVDrvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note: When NRBTS/NRCELL/actPrachC2Scs15kHzTddFr1 is set to TRUE, PRACH scs is set to 15 kHz in TDD FR1 (refer 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_13950_replaced([11279220](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUps_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).In this case related calculation of prach occasion shall be based on cell UL slot.

OutPut:

The beamID of rach occasion which the preamble is received.

In case of [CB009543-A] TDD [End CB009543-A] supercell mode enabled according to 5G_L2_7884_replaced([11153463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24uFzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the beamId derived from rach occasion represents the associated L1 subcell. There is a mapping for supercell between beamId and pair of DL L1 subcellId and UL L1 subcellId of given L1 subcell, refer to 5G_UP_1851_replaced([11375846](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueQLIfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In case of Pusch is 8Rx or 16Rx(numOfRxPusch=8 or 16, see 5G_L2_16925_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the first raBeamId selection is the same as legacy. The secondRaBeamId selection is the same as ulSubCellType=C4. See 5G_UP_ALG_3388_replaced([11152417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

• DL: the usage of beamId is same as legacy after raBeamId/secondRaBeamId selection.

• UL PUCCH 4RX (numOfRxPucch=4, see 5G_L2_16925_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): the usage of beamId is raBeamId + secondRaBeamId, the same as legacy ulSubCellType C4.

• UL PUCCH/PUSCH 8RX (numOfRxPucch=8/numOfRxPusch=8, see 5G_L2_16925_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))): the usage of beamId is raBeamId + highCorrelation beam + two half sector beam. See 5G_L2_9602_replaced([11155694](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466E_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_L2_12901_replaced([11155736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2466G_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In case of Pusch is 4Rx(numOfRxPusch=4, see 5G_L2_16925_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the first raBeamId selection is the same as legacy. The secondRaBeamId selection is the same as ulSubCellType=C4. See 5G_UP_ALG_3388_replaced([11152417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DL/UL: the usage of beamId is same as legacy ulSubCellType C4 after raBeamId/secondRaBeamId selection.

---

#### 2.4.3.3.2 beamGroup selection (ID: 11153539)
##### 2.4.3.3.2.0-1 beamGroup selection beamGroup selection in FR2 SA (ID: 11153545)

**2.4.3.3.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11153550`)

beamGroup selection In FR2 SA, 5G-L2-PS instance, shall proceed accordingly to 5G_UP_ALG_20447_replaced([11113612](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpcHW_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for initial beamGroup selection procedure.

In FR2 SA CA, if 5G-L2-PS instance receives PsUser_UserModifyReq with cellConfigurationForModification.pdschConfig.beamGroupSize set to 16 for PCell during SCell Addition procedure, then 5G-L2-PS instance (according to 5G_UP_ALG_23110_replaced([11113799](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpd8jPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall:

• calculate beamGroup and targetSsbBeam, based on received pdschConfig.beamGroupSize;

• fill and send PsUser_UserModifyResp with calculated beamGroup and targetSsbBeam;

• not send PsUser_BeamGroupChangeInd according to 5G_L2_IF_531_replaced([10977540](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VNSZvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), because beamGroup and targetSsbBeam are already sent in PsUser_UserModifyResp.

---

##### 2.4.3.3.2.0-2 beamGroup selection Mapping of the Initial Beam to a Beam Group (ID: 11153556)

**2.4.3.3.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11153562`)

beamGroup selection L2-PS shall store mapping tables for beam groups including beam ids and configured beam set as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2220_replaced([11113602](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpcHWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.3.3.3 Beam tracking and beam refinement (ID: 11153567)
##### 2.4.3.3.3.1 SSB and Refine Beam Management (ID: 11153572)

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.1 Configuration (ID: 11153577)

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-1 Configuration SSB Beam (ID: 11153581)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11153607`)

Configuration 5G-L2-PS receives SSB related parameters in PsCell_CellGroupSetupReq(5G_L2_IF_3117_replaced([10966509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UPCB_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• *numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks and *ssBurstSetPeriod**

5G-L2-PS also receives cell level parameters from BfSetup struct in PsCell_CellSetupReq(5G_L2_IF_61_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• *beamSet*

• *beamWeightVector and *ulBeamIdPatternIdMapping**

• *fullSectorBeamPatternId, halfSectorBeamPatternId and quarterSectorBeamPatternId*

• *ulSsbCorrelatedBeamSet, numberOfRxPucch*

• *ulRefinementCorrelatedBeamSet*

• *mMimoPSBeamWeightVector.patternId*

If Beamforming is enabled according to 5G_L2_7884_replaced([11153463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24uFzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G-L2-PS shall

• Store *numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks and *ssBurstSetPeriod for SS block transmission.**

• Store corresponding *beamSet which include parameters related to the configuration of beams, see 5G_UP_ALG_1930_replaced([11111654](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpPTCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_1931_replaced([11111662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpPTC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

• Store *beamWeightVector and *ulBeamIdPatternIdMapping which identify the mapping between beamId and patternId for DL and UL, see 5G_UP_ALG_1939_replaced([11111782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpQhIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

NOTE: the mapping also includes refine beams if beam refinement is enabled.

• Store refine beam to the corresponding SSB beam according to *beamWeightVector, which will be used for beam extension defined in 5G_UP_ALG_21306_replaced([11113376](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpbgTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if beam refinement is enabled.*

• Store *fullSectorBeamPatternId, *halfSectorBeamPatternId, quarterSectorBeamPatternId for sector beam usage, see 5G_UP_ALG_2037_replaced([11112372](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

• Store *ulSsbCorrelatedBeamSet/numberOfRxPucch for SSB beam usage. For each SSB (coarse) beam, CPlane will calculate two highest correlation beam. L2PS use legacy SSB beam and ulSsbCorrelatedBeamSet to extend one SSB beam or two SSB beam to four beam for 8RX. See 5G_UP_ALG_2032_replaced([11112298](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

• Store *ulRefinementCorrelatedBeamSet for SSB/refine beam usage. For each SSB (coarse)/refine beam, CPlane will calculate two highest correlation beams(5G_CP_ARC_ALG_11171_replaced([11297028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqGFwfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). L2PS use legacy SSB/refine beams and ulRefinementCorrelatedBeamSet to extend one SSB/refine beam or two SSB/refine beams to four beams for PUSCH scheduling for 8RX. See 5G_UP_ALG_21306_replaced([11113376](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpbgTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

If **[CB009543-A] TDD **[End CB009543-A] supercell is enabled according to 5G_L2_7884_replaced([11153463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24uFzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G-L2-PS shall****

• Store *numberOfTransmittedSsBlocks and *ssBurstSetPeriod for SS block transmission, see 5G_UP_ALG_4106_replaced([11173741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1d_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

• Store corresponding *beamSet which include parameters related to the configuration of beams, see 5G_UP_ALG_13175_replaced([11111819](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpQhLfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

[CB014301-AA] 5G-L2-PS may receive beam related parameters in PsCell_BeamConfigUpdateReq 5G_L2_IF [12536044: PsCell_BeamConfigUpdateReq](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_wLou8R9PEfCQJ5daVHVkgg) after online modification of beamset NIDD parameters[End CB014301-AA]

---

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-2 Configuration Refine Beam (ID: 11153614)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11153618`)

Configuration [CNI-84537] 5G-L2-PS receives Refine beam parameter in PsCell_CellSetupReq(5G_L2_IF_61_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• nrBtsBeamRefinementP2

If Digital Beamforming is enabled according to 5G_L2_7884_replaced([11153463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24uFzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and nrBtsBeamRefinementP2 is true, 5G-L2-PS shall enable beam refinement:

• Apply CSI-RS transmission for beam refinement, see 5G_UP_ALG_21843_replaced([11159700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktLX1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

5G-L2-PS receives UE specific refine beam parameter from IE cellConfiguration in PsUser_UserSetupReq(5G_L2_IF_389_replaced([10974645](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U9ayPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or PsUser_UserModifyReq(5G_L2_IF_453_replaced([10976074](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VEvgvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

• appliedBeamRefinementP2

If Beam refinement is enabled and appliedBeamRefinementP2 is true, 5G-l2-PS shall apply beam refinement for this UE:

• Send request for Beam management based on CRI-RSRP, see 5G_UP_ALG_3604_replaced([11159975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktNNBPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

• Receive response for CRI Beam report, see 5G_UP_ALG_3617_replaced([11160013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktNNC_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

• Filter beam power measurement and do beam selection, see 5G_UP_ALG_1999_replaced([11112098](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpS9avvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE:

• For digital beamforming, SSB and Refine beams configuration may be different for PCell and SCell, 5G-L2-PS shall deal wtih them separately.

• If appliedBeamRefinementP2 is updated on P(S)Cell due to SCell addtion/release, special procedure during the reconfiguration transient phase need to be applied, see 5G_UP_ALG_4196_replaced([11174380](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kubVCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_4826_replaced([11195069](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv5UqfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). [END CNI-84537]

---

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-3 Configuration Beam Fallback and Sweeping (ID: 11153626)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11153630`)

Configuration [CNI-84537] 5G-L2-PS shall set appliedUlBeamSweeping, appliedUlBeamFallback, and appliedDlBeamProbingBeforeChange according to 5G_UP_ALG_1888_replaced([11111451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[END CNI-84537]

---

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.2 Beam fallback for UL (ID: 11153637)

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.2.0-1 Beam fallback for UL UL Beam probing and fallback (ID: 11153642)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.2.0-1.0-6**  (ID: `12591677`)

Beam fallback for UL If *appliedUlBeamFallback == TRUE (5G_L2_1848_replaced([11212918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-ogjvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),5G_L2_1851_replaced([11212942](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-pHkvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):*

5G-L2-PS shall conduct,

1. If a target beam was not defined by a beam report, the procedures of uplink beam fallback as defined by this requirement (5G_UP_ALG_2041_replaced([11112416](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZr_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall not be conducted.

2. If dlMimoMode is CL 8x4MIMO,beam for UL receiver shall still be from Beam report, the procedures of uplink beam fallback as defined by this requirement and retrigger UL fall back procedure defined in 5G_L2_8179_replaced([11153750](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24wiAvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall still be conducted based on SSB/refined beam changing from beam report.

3. If UEs which are configured with periodic SRS and state machine for BM is NOT under the state of SRS_BM_AGED (see 5G_L2_5339_replaced([11153981](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24xwKvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the UL beam fallback and probing algorithms shall be skipped because UL beams are selected based on periodic SRS but not beam reports from UEs.

4. If FR1 SCell with UL configured according to 5G_UP_ALG_2698_replaced([11120363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqP_rPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), UL beam fallback and probing algorithms shall be conducted.

5. If Pusch is 4Rx(numOfRxPusch=4, see 5G_L2_16925_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

5G-L2-PS shall conduct UL Beam probing and fallback as in 5G_L2_8177_replaced([11153681](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24v69fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

else if Pusch is 8Rx(numOfRxPusch=8, see 5G_L2_16925_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

5G-L2-PS shall conduct UL Beam probing and fallback as in 5G_L2_13275_replaced([11153692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24v69_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

else Pusch is 16Rx(numOfRxPusch=16, see 5G_L2_16925_replaced([11141685](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_23qV0_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

5G-L2-PS shall conduct UL Beam probing and fallback as in 5G_L2_17762_replaced([11153703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24v6-fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

else

****a.In case of Probing****

The old UL source beam ID shall be stored in UE context before the UL beam changes. Instantaneous SINR values of a) the last scheduled UL slot before and b) the first scheduled UL slot after an updated beam ID is applied shall be measured and stored in the UE context.

**NOTE: an updated beam ID shall be applied when a MAC CE PDCCH TCI state indication is HARQ ACKed or finally HARQ NACKed, the timing refer to 5G_UP_ALG_29442_replaced([11112392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZrPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

**NOTE: updated beam PMI ID application shall not be impacted by MAC CE PDCCH TCI state indication is HARQ ACKed or finally HARQ NACKed.**

If the UL SINR value of the new beam ID is better than the value of the old beam ID or the PUSCH on old beam is DTX'ed, then the new beam ID shall be applied further as serving UL beam for PUCCH Format 3, PUSCH, and to AP-SRS for SU-MIMO. Normalized SINR values, as in 5G_UP_ALG_2048_replaced([11112469](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpWAuvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), shall be compared.

****b.In case of UL Beam Fallback****

If SINR of the new candidate beam is equal or lower than the SINR value of the old beam, **or the PUSCH on new candidate beam is DTX'ed, the old beam ID shall be applied in next slots for PUCCH, PUSCH, and to AP-SRS for SU-MIMO. If the UL beam falls back to the old beam ID, this shall be conducted directly in the next slot without any latency and without signaling to the UE.**

Endif

6. If UCA cell (5G_UP_921_replaced([11353688](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucBWiPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for FR1-TDD, eCPRI, C4, appliedUlBeamFallback shall be FALSE (5G_L2_8050_replaced([11153626](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24vT7PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and UL beam selection is performed according to 5G_UP_ALG_2212_replaced([11113055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpaSI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and does not rely on beam report.

Note:

 Any UL symbols between above two UL slots, e.g., PUCCH symbols in special slots, shall use the serving beam as defined without the PR4830646. That old beam ID shall be applied before the updated beam ID is applied. The new beam ID shall be applied after that point in time.

 UL slots used for (re-)probing are not considered by to OLLA and ILLA, see 5G_UP_ALG_3078_replaced([11130719](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krANl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_3060_replaced([11129299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kq78IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and 5G_UP_ALG_3153_replaced([11137866](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krcScfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 5G-L2-PS shall apply beam pattern Ids to physical channels including Long PUCCH as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2031_replaced([11112294](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUyl_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and in 5G_UP_ALG_2215_replaced([11113083](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpaSLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 PM counters NR Beamforming/UL_SERV_BEAM_ID_BEAM_xx (M55305C010xx) shall be updated with current serving beam for all probing allocations (i.e. not with the beam which is probed)**.**

 Regarding any DTX of PUSCH mentioned in this requirement, the corresponding response is not applicable if DTX determined for any of the following and UE is configured for skippable grants (5G_UP_ALG_4839_replaced([11195286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kv7J1fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

o skippable Proactive grant

o fake SR (SR indication received from L2-HI)

o TA Grant

o UL grant after a zero BSR was received

[CB014005-A]

Note: In any UL beam probing and fallback case mentioned above, for beam report with ReportQuantity::ssb_Index_RSRP or ReportQuantity::cri_RSRP for UL, stop/restart the UL beam probing and rollback when cell beam update as [12592060: Beam probing stop and re-start](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_MFNe8DbrEfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

[End CB014005-A]

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.2.0-3 Beam fallback for UL UL Beam probing and fallback for Pusch 8Rx (ID: 11153692)

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.3 Beam fallback for DL (ID: 11153764)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.3.0-3**  (ID: `11153774`)

Beam fallback for DL UL Beam Probing Before DL Beam Change

---

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.4 UL beam sweeping (ID: 11153781)

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.4.0-1 UL beam sweeping 1.Trigger for beam sweeping. (ID: 11153786)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11153798`)

UL beam sweeping when appliedUlBeamSweeping == TRUE (5G_UP_ALG_1888_replaced([11111451](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))):

If UE’s CSI report for beam management received from the message of PuschReceiveRespU is DTX’ed (5G_UP_ALG_22403_replaced([11187538](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvbanvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and/or its CRC failed continuously for more than rdUlBeamSweepingTriggerCounts defined in 5G_L2_1241_replaced([11183642](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27lMhvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

5G-L2-PS shall conduct SSB beam sweeping until a target beam is able to receive the CSI report for beam management in a NON-DTX’ed manner.

Note:

For UEs which are configured with periodic SRS and state machine for BM is NOT under the state of SRS_BM_AGED (see 5G_UP_ALG_2136_replaced([11154035](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksqac_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the UL beam sweeping shall NOT be triggered

UL beams are selected based on periodic SRS but not on beam reports from UEs

 In case ulSubcellType is C4 [CB007595-C] or C4_2[End CB007595-C] and UE is configured with 2 SSB beams and beam refinement is disabled for the UE and periodic SRS is not configured for the UE, UL beam sweeping shall not be triggered.

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.5 Beam handling When Cell Beam Update (ID: 12585977)

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.5.0-1 Beam handling When Cell Beam Update Beam measurement reset and beam selection (ID: 12585978)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.5.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12585979`)

Beam handling When Cell Beam Update When a UE receives first valid beam report with ReportQuantity::ssb_Index_RSRP or ReportQuantity::cri_RSRP for a serving cell after the cell's down tilt change recorded in [12582669: Handling of cell down tilt change](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0nFmoTSEEfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), the measurement for beam shall be reset, and beam shall be selected according to [11112143: Beam change in case of SSB/refined beam set online update](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpTkc_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9). Also beam toggling shall be reset according to [11112172: Beam toggling recording for SSB/refined Beam](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpTkevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

##### 2.4.3.3.3.1.5.0-2 Beam handling When Cell Beam Update Beam probing stop and re-start (ID: 12592060)

**2.4.3.3.3.1.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12592061`)

Beam handling When Cell Beam Update When a UE receives first valid beam report with ReportQuantity::ssb_Index_RSRP or ReportQuantity::cri_RSRP for a serving cell after the cell's down tilt change recorded in [12582669: Handling of cell down tilt change](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_0nFmoTSEEfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), select a beam based on the report directly according to [12585978: Beam measurement reset and beam selection](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WSZXcDX1EfCKaNlcHOQWmg?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), stop the possible beam probing and rollback procedure and initialize the probing beam data. The beam probing and roll back can only start again till next beam change.

##### 2.4.3.3.3.2 Periodic code book SRS based beam management for UL (ID: 11153909)

##### 2.4.3.3.3.2.0-1 Periodic code book SRS based beam management for UL Configuration (ID: 11153915)

**2.4.3.3.3.2.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13440930`)

Periodic code book SRS based beam management for UL [Before CNI-157588-H]At reception of Cell setup message, 5G-L2-PS shall check if uplink SRS based beam management is enabled or not.

If *actUlSrsBm in *bfSetup structure (see 5G_L2_IF_115_replaced([10968891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UaBKvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) of **PsCell_CellSetupReq (see 5G_L2_IF_61_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is *true:
 [CNI-157588-H]
 Preconditions: The requirement applies only to the configuration where uplink SRS based beam management is enabled. Upon reception of PsCell_CellSetupReq message the value of *actUlSrsBm in *bfSetup structure (see [10968891](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UaBKvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be equal to *True.
 [End CNI-157588-H]********

5G-L2-PS shall apply periodic codebook SRS based uplink beam management for UEs which are configured with periodic SRS resources with usage as CODEBOOK[CNI-157588-H] (see [10975309](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VAeFfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and [10976392](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VF9rfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))[End CNI-157588-H].

5G-L2-PS shall store corresponding *DftBeamSet [CNI-157588-H](https://systeminsights.ext.net.nokia.com/see%20[10969071](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UaoOPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9))[End CNI-157588-H] identified by *dftBeamSetId in *ulSrsBmConfig[CNI-157588-H] (see [10969099](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_87tzsPu3Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CNI-157588-H], which includes the horizontal DFT beams according to *dftBeamWeightH, *numOfDftBeamForUlSrsBmH, and vertical DFT beams according to *dftBeamWeightV, *numOfDftBeamForUlSrsBmV.*******

[Before CNI-157588-H]The 2D-DFT beams are Kronecker product of horizontal and vertical DFT beams. **NOTE: The DFT beams are used by UL beam selection algorithm (see 5G_L2_3589_replaced([11154211](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24y-SvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).[End CNI-157588-H]**

[CB015122-A]
 If actSrsBmCalcInL1 is set to True (see [10966124](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UMlxPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)), then DFT beam selection algorithm is moved to 5G-L1-DU (see [11154028](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksqacvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)). 5G-L2-PS shall store SRS beam parameters received from 5G-L1-DU in message UlData_SrsBeamCalcUlReceiveRespPs (see [13456660](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2u4hYC0NEfGxldjOZQLezQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).

Else
 -[End CB015122-A] 5G-L2-PS shall calculate correlations between each horizontal DFT beams and store to a lookup table. 5G-L2-PS shall also calculate correlations between each vertical DFT beams and store to a lookup table. **NOTE: The lookup tables are used to speed up the second-best DFT beam selection process by directly getting the correlation of two DFT beams from these tables (See 5G_L2_4042_replaced([11154242](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24zlUPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).**

- If *isDftBeamsetConf is True, the configured UL beam set is 2D-DFT beam, 5G-L2-PS shall store the start pattern id of 2D-DFT beams (*dftBeamStartPatternId). The pattern id of an 2D-DFT beam (*dftBeamPatternId) identified by corresponding *dftBeamIndexH, *dftBeamIndexV can be calculated as:
 dftBeamPatternId = *dftBeamStartPatternId + *numOfDftBeamForUlSrsBmH * *dftBeamIndexV + *dftBeamIndexH.
 - If *isDftBeamsetConf is False, the configured UL beam set is GoB beam, 5G-L2-PS shall store the mapping table (*dftGoBMapping) between the 2D-DFT beam and the pattern ID of GoB beam.***********

[CB015122-A]
 end
 [End CB015122-A]

**NOTE 1: Parameters *dftBeamSetId, isDftBeamsetConf, dftGoBMapping are IEs of *ulSrsBmConfig in the *bfSetup structure.*****

[Before CNI-157588-H]**NOTE 2: The *DftBeamSet is one element of *dftBeamSetList*in *bfSetup structure, which has unique *dftBeamSetId and dftBeamWeightH, *numOfDftBeamForUlSrsBmH, *dftBeamWeightV, *numOfDftBeamForUlSrsBmV, dftBeamStartPatternId are the IEs of DftBeamSet[CNI-157588-H]**********

**NOTE 2: The 2D-DFT beams are Kronecker product of horizontal and vertical DFT beams. The DFT beams are used by UL beam selection algorithm (see [11154211](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24y-SvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).[End CNI-157588-H].**
