### 2.4.3.8 UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam (ID: 11156189)
#### 2.4.3.8.0-1 UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam Maintain of dominant UL Beam for UE (ID: 11156193)

**2.4.3.8.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13341693`)

UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam [CB008577-E] For TDD: [End CB008577-E]

5G-L2-PS need to maintain dominant UL DFT beam info for each UEs in Cell, including bestH, bestV, the beam updated Xsfn.

If UE configured with period codeBook SRS:

1) if valid DFT beam decoded by SrsEo, 5G-L2-PS need to save latest best DFT beam for the UE:

'ulSrsBmBestBeamIndexH' will saved to bestH, and

'ulSrsBmBestBeamIndexV' will saved to bestV, the two IE defined in 'UlSrsBeamSelectionInd' msg.

'validBeamUpdateXsfn' will update as current Xsfn

2) if receive 'UlSrsBeamSelectionInd' msg with srsBmState = aged from SrsEo, and last DFT beam is valid:

5G-L2-PS need to start a timer for the UE, the timer length equal to 'ulAoAMonitorWindow'.

2.1) if there is valid DFT beam decoded during the timer, 5G-L2-PS need to stop the timer, and save the valid DFT beam as Step 1);

2.1) if there is no valid DFT beam decoded during the timer, 5G-L2-PS need to set bestH/bestV/validBeamUpdateXsfn to invalid value after timeout.

Dominant UL SSB/refine beam should always be the primary SSB/refine beam used for PUSCH transmission.

Note: If 'ulAoAMonitorWindow' is reconfigured by PsCell_CellReconfigurationReq, timer has been started before reconfiguration will keep using the old value. Only timer started after reconfiguration will use the new parameter.

[CB008577-E] For FDD: [End CB008577-E]

5G-L2-PS need to use UE's latest maintaining PUSCH beam type (PMI , refinement of fixed beam#4, or fixed beam #4 ), with related Beam's bestH, bestV.

If UE with valid PMI beam , then related full panel PMI beam's bestH and bestV shall be recorded.

Else if UE with refinement of fixed beam#4, then the one is related fixed beam#4 's bestH and bestV shall be recorded.

else UE with fixed beam#4, then the default full sector beam's bestH and bestV shall be recorded.

endif

ENDIF

Note : For FDD Beamforming (NRCELL/actBeamforming = True and NRCELL.cellTechnology is equal to 'FDD')

1, the ulAoAMonitorWindow will not be configured to L2PS. Always the latest maintaining PUSCH channel related beam's bestH and bestV shall be used.

2, Because the DL beam's doaInfo will only be configured to L2PS, then L2PS shall map the PUSCH channel related beam to DL beam first.

CP2 refer [11113859: Determination of dominant UL beam for Positioning](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpejnvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

Informative refer how CP calculate AoA :[11290451: LCS AoA of PMI beams generation](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqCbb_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) , [11289401: LCS AoA Value extracting and transforming for SSB and/or refinement beam](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqAmMfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

---

#### 2.4.3.8.0-2 UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam Response UL AOA to CPRT (ID: 11156212)

**2.4.3.8.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `13341717`)

UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam When received PsUser_RanMeasurementReq with 'angleOfArrivalNrRequired' equal to true,

**[CB008577-E] For TDD:**[End CB008577-E] 5G-L2-PS need to check whether there is valid DFT beam bestH and bestV saved for the UE in the past time duration of 'ulAoaMonitorWindow', if exist valid DFT beams, only the latest DFT beam corresponding aoaH and aoaV will response to CPRT. **If there has no valid DFT beams, dominant UL SSB/refine beam should be used for AoA measurement and corresponding aoaH and aoaV will be replied to CPRT.******

**[CB008577-E] For FDD: **[End CB008577-E] 5G-L2-PS will response the recorded latest beam's aoaH and aoaV of PUSCH channel to CPRT.****

Note: aoaH and aoaV for SSB/refine [CB008577-E ] /full sector/ default fix beam#4 / DL PMI/ [End CB008577-E] beam is received in [Before CB008577-E ] PsCell_CellSsbRefineBeamDoaInd [CB008577-E ] PsCell_CellSsbRefinePmiBeamDoaInd [End CB008577-E] from CPRT. Refer to 5G_L2_IF_5996_replaced([10974196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2U6Xefu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

#### 2.4.3.8.0-3 UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam Response UL SSB beam Id to CPRT in case of supercell/subsector (ID: 11156229)

**2.4.3.8.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11156234`)

UL Angle of Arrival based on UL beam Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_25920_replaced([11113880](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpfKo_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.3.9 FDD Supercell management (ID: 11156241)
#### 2.4.3.9.0-1 FDD Supercell management Initial serving subcell in UL (ID: 11156245)

**2.4.3.9.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156251`)

FDD Supercell management L2-PS shall select the initial serving subcell in UL for a UE based on the PRACH based serving subcell selection procedure as per [11170357](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26JCG_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).Initial serving subcell shall act as serving subcell for the UE until the first SINR report is processed from PUCCH F3 response from the L1-UL.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-2 FDD Supercell management Serving subcell in UL (ID: 11156255)

**2.4.3.9.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11156261`)

FDD Supercell management L2-PS shall select the serving subcell in the UL for a UE based on the SINR reports recevied in PUCCH F3 as described in [12582938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GtKjwDScEfCKaNlcHOQWmg) and [12584579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_1-sdQTVJEfCKaNlcHOQWmg)

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-3 FDD Supercell management UL message scheduling (ID: 11156266)

**2.4.3.9.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11156270`)

FDD Supercell management L2-PS shall request UL messages for PRACH **[CB009543-B] and PUCCH F3 **[End CB009543-B] from all the UL subcells(serving and non-serving subcells) of the UE. All other UL messages are requested only from UE's UL serving subcell.****

Rationale : As explained in 5G_UP_13262_replaced([11353566](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucAvc_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), PRACH reception and **[CB009543-B] PUCCH F3 measurements **[End CB009543-B] shall be performed on all subcells only to facilitate initial serving subcell selection **[CB009543-B] and serving subcell selection respectively **[End CB009543-B]. Other UL message receptions over PUSCH/PUCCH shall be only through UL serving subcell of the UE.********

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-4 FDD Supercell management **PUCCH reception request updates** (ID: 11156276)

**2.4.3.9.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11156282`)

FDD Supercell management L2-PS shall perform PUCCH reception only on UE's initial UL serving subcell or UL serving subcell. L2-PS shall set isServingSubcell to true for all PUCCH formats for TDD supercell, FDD supercell and non-supercell cases while sending UlData_PucchReceiveReqL1sw to L1-UL.

[CB009543-B]

Only PUCCH F3 is requested for reception from all UL subcells. Other PUCCH formats are requested reception only from UE's UL serving subcell.

If a UE is scheduled for PUCCH F3 resources on its serving subcell, the same PUCCH resources are allocated on all the non-serving subcells. This way there will be multiple UlData_PucchReceiveReqL1sw messages(One for serving subcell and one for each non-serving subcell) carrying the same UE's information. Each UlData_PucchReceiveReqL1sw also multiplexes multiple UEs requesting PUCCH F3 and other formats towards the same subcell. All other requirements regarding PUCCH reception as per [11150467](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_D7stgPvMEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) are applicable for FDD supercell.

[CB009543-C1]

In CA cases PUCCH F3 resource allocation shall be skipped for a UE if its UE's non-serving subcell over its SCell.

[End CB009543-C1]

The parameter ' isServingSubcell ' (within UlData_PucchReceiveReqL1sw.PucchResourceAllocationConfigL1sw) is used to indicate the L1-UL about the UE's serving subcell applicability. This IE shall indicate whether PUCCH resource processing should be Decode+Measurement on serving subcell(isServingSubcell =true) or only Measurement on non-serving subcell(isServingSubcell =false).

L2-PS shall set 'isServingSubcell' as indicated below

1. PUCCH F3 in FDD supercell for UE's UL serving subcell, 'isServingSubcell' is set to true

2. PUCCH F3 in FDD supercell for UE's UL non serving subcell, 'isServingSubcell' is set to false

3. Other PUCCH formats in FDD supercell for UE's UL serving subcell, 'isServingSubcell' is set to true

4. All PUCCH formats in TDD supercell for UE's UL serving subcell, 'isServingSubcell' is set to true

5. All PUCCH formats in non-supercell (normal cell) for UE's UL serving subcell, 'isServingSubcell' is set to true

[End CB009543-B]

[CB009543-C1]

Note: Requesting UlData_PucchReceiveReqL1sw for non-serving subcells are valid only when P-CSI reporting for the Pcell scheduled interval. Otherwise, non serving subcell related measurements request shall be ignored either Scell P-CSI or HARQ Feedback via PUCCH F3 Reporting Interval.

[End CB009543-C1]

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-5 FDD Supercell management PUCCH reception response handling (ID: 12582938)

**2.4.3.9.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `12582944`)

FDD Supercell management L2-PS receives UlData_PucchReceiveRespL1sw from all the requested subcells. PUCCH Responses with F3 containing SINR values shall be used to select the best UL serving subcell. L2-PS maintains these parameters to be used for filtered SINR computation and subcell switch decision making

i. Filtered SINR list - Maintained per UE, the list of latest computed filtered SINR values for all the configured subcells(one SINR value for one subcell). This list is used across processing cycles(slots) and is initialized with value 0dB.

ii. Target subcell ID - Maintained per UE and used only within a processing cycle(slot). Holds the target subcell ID until the subcell switch is completed.The value for all the UEs is initialized to 'Invalid subcell ID' before the first UlData_PucchReceiveRespL1sw is handled in a processing cycle(slot).

iii. Best SINR - Maintained per UE, its the temporary highest value of filtered SINR across all the configured subcells within a processing cycle(slot). The value for all the UEs is initialized to -23dB before the first UlData_PucchReceiveRespL1sw is handled in a processing cycle(slot).

iv. ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch - Maintained per cell, the list of UEs which have a possibility to switch their serving subcell. This list gets updated and flushed every processing cycle(slot).

For each subcell's decoded control data (pucchResources mapped to a UE) within each UlData_PucchReceiveRespL1sw containing format 3 in every slot, L2-PS shall follow these steps

1. Computes the new filtered SINR of the UE and corresponding subcell as per [11113918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vpITkfvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

2. Stores/Updates the filtered SINR list with the new filtered SINR as per [11113918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vpITkfvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

3. If the new filtered SINR is greater than the Best SINR,

a. Update the Best SINR with the new filtered SINR

b. Update UE's Target subcell ID with the subcell ID corresponding to the new filtered SINR

c. Add the UE to "ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch" list if it was not added earlier. Else skip adding the UE again.

After handling all the received UlData_PucchReceiveRespL1sw, L2-PS shall use the above parameters for subcell switching decision during the post processing as described in [12584579](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_1-sdQTVJEfCKaNlcHOQWmg)

Note:

1. New filtered SINR is the value 'SINR(u,s)' depicted on LHS of the equation in [11113918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vpITkfvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

1. 'measuredSINR(u,s)' depicted in [11113918](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vpITkfvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) is the SINR received in the first element of SINR[] in message UlData_PucchReceiveRespL1sw

2. L2-PS shall follow the above mentioned steps only for PUCCH F3 in FDD supercell. All other PUCCH formats to follow non-supercell handling.

3. L2-PS does not wait for a response from all the configured UL subcells. Only the received UlData_PucchReceiveRespL1sw messages within a processing cycle(slot) are handled for serving subcell switching decisions.

4. Above steps are applicable for UlData_PucchReceiveRespL1sw received from UE's serving and non-serving subcells

5. SINR has a valid range between -23dB to 40dB. If out of range SINR is reported, L2-PS shall truncate the SINR value the nearest valid value before using it to compute filtered SINR. Example : (i) SINR reported as -25dB, L2-PS considers it as -23dB for filtered SINR computation. (ii) SINR reported as 45dB, L2-PS considers it as 40dB for computing filtered SINR

6. All samples of SINR are considered within the UlData_PucchReceiveRespL1sw message irrespective of their CRC and DTX status. SINRs from samples with DTX or CRC_NOK are also considered valid for filtered SINR computation.

7. L2-PS shall limit the lowest value for an entry in Filtered SINR list as -23dB.

8. L2-PS shall limit the highest value for an entry in Filtered SINR list as 40dB.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-6 FDD Supercell management UL Serving subcell switch selection (ID: 12584579)

**2.4.3.9.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `12584580`)

FDD Supercell management UE's UL serving subcell needs to be reviewed for a possible switch during the post-processing. L2-PS shall use the SINR values (processed as per[12582938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GtKjwDScEfCKaNlcHOQWmg)) in the below mentioned steps to check serving subcell switch for each UE in ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch.

For every UE in the ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch L2-PS shall check

1. If (TargetSubcellSINR is greater than ThresholdSINR) and (Target subcell ID is not equal to current subcell ID), Consider the UE eligible for UL serving subcell switch and indicate UL scheduler about the target subcell ID. Add an entry in PsInternal_DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate.ueSubcellSwitchSuperCellFdd with UE's rnti and Target subcell ID (Set ueSubcellSwitchSuperCellFdd.rnti = UE's rnti, ueSubcellSwitchSuperCellFdd.targetUlServingSubcellId = Target subcell ID).

2. Update L2 tracing event with UE specific data (rnti of trace data mapped to UE's rnti)

a. ulServingSubcellSwitchDataSuperCellFdd.targetUlServingSubcellId = Target subcell ID if condition at step 1 was true. Else use UE's current UL serving subcell ID

b. ulServingSubcellSwitchDataSuperCellFdd.subcellId[0..n] = subcellId[0..n] configured for FDD supercell

c. ulServingSubcellSwitchDataSuperCellFdd.filteredSINR[0..n] = UE's filtered SINR[0..n]

1. Trigger the L2 tracing event ulServingSubcellSwitchDataSuperCellFdd.

2. Update Target subcell ID with 'Invalid subcell ID'

3. Update Best SINR with value -23dB

4. Remove the UE from ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch list

Note:

1. TargetSubcellSINR = SINR in the UE's Filtered SINR list (processed as per[12582938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GtKjwDScEfCKaNlcHOQWmg)) corresponding to the Target subcell ID

2. ThresholdSINR = SINR in the UE's Filtered SINR list (processed as per[12582938](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GtKjwDScEfCKaNlcHOQWmg)) corresponding to the current serving subcell ID + *rdSubcellSwitchSINRThreshold is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_28478_replaced([11114169](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpiN-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))*

3. Condition mentioned in step 1 effectively checks for the subcell switch criteria mentioned in [11113928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_vt--YPvIEe-AqvopbP1qhQ). Also implies the UE does not need a subcell switch if condition check at step 1 fails

4. ' n ' refers to configured number of subcells in FDD supercell. Refer [12654808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_HiAKoEcfEfC0Ye3M8ARUWQ) for details about the trace event IEs. ~~The remaining elements in the trace data are filled with default values as indicated in [12654808](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_HiAKoEcfEfC0Ye3M8ARUWQ)~~

5. L2 tracing event ulServingSubcellSwitchDataSuperCellFdd shall be updated and triggered for every UE which is part of ShortlistedUesForSubcellSwitch

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-7 FDD Supercell management UL Serving subcell switch execution (ID: 12636880)

**2.4.3.9.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `12636882`)

FDD Supercell management Upon receiving the intra scheduler message PsInternal_DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate, L2-PS shall update the UE's UL serving subcell ID with the ueSubcellSwitchSuperCellFdd.targetUlServingSubcellId. All the UL messages to be handled by serving subcell are now handled by the new UL serving subcell. L2-PS shall reset CFO and TA values after switching to new UL serving subcell.

1. Carrier frequency offset(CFO) metric rrmAvgCfoPrevious is reset to 0 as per [11150991](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_GXnWUPvMEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

2. Timing advance (TA) metric is reset to 0 as per [11150033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_B4anIPvMEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Note:

1. L2-PS uses the new UL serving subcell for issuing UL grants for the new transmissions and ongoing retransmissions over PUSCH. It updates PuschHarqConfig.freshHarqTrans according to the transmission type (True = New/Initial transmission, False = Retransmission), PuschHarqConfig.absoluteHarqProcessIndex value is not modified and PuschDecoderConfig.rvIndex according to legacy algorithms (No special handling just for FDD Supercell)

2. Due to subcell switch, the ongoing retransmissions(Grants on PUSCH) are now switched to new subcell. This can cause the packet drop as the new subcell may have partial data regarding the retransmissions and can lead to decoding failures.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-8 FDD Supercell management Pattern ID handling for DL and UL messages (ID: 12519755)

**2.4.3.9.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `12519976`)

FDD Supercell management In case of FDD supercell is enabled, 'Pattern Id' is set to 0 for all relevant UL and DL messages towards L1.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-9 FDD Supercell management PMaxSubcell Handling for EIRP measurements and control (ID: 12700607)

**2.4.3.9.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `12700608`)

FDD Supercell management PMaxSubcell is the maximum power transmitted for each subcell. This value is received in Cell setup as an integer value. However L2-Ps converts it to dBm by dividing by 10. L2-PS stores values of these in a local array PMaxSubcell[] and uses them only when EIRP is enabled either through cell setup or cell reconfiguration.

---

#### 2.4.3.9.0-10 FDD Supercell management Weakest segment for EIRP (ID: 12700618)

**2.4.3.9.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `12700620`)

FDD Supercell management Each subcell represents a segment and PMaxSubcells[] are mapped to their respective segments in EIRP calculations. Segment with the least value of "Effective_PMaxSubcell" shall be marked as the weakest segment.

Effective_PMaxSubcell[i] in dBm = PMaxSubcell[i] in dBm - PowerReductionFactor[i] in dB

Where i is the segment index

In case if multiple segments have the same Effective_PMaxSubcell, the one with lower subcell index is chosen as the weakest segment.

---

#### 2.4.3.10.0-1 FDD Beamforming L3 Call Beam ID for DL (ID: 12697999)

**2.4.3.10.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12697996`)

FDD Beamforming L3 Call If actBeamformingFdd (see [10967202: PsCell_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), the beam applied for DL channels/signals is as bellow according to [11113948: FDD mMIMO beamforming PatternID](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpfxufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9):

SSB: ssbBeamPatternId[0] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9), 1 beam for only 1 SSB as per [11094949: Mapping of antenna ports to antenna elements for FDD](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmhWN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

CSI-RS for tracking: keep the same as SSB ssbBeamPatternId[0] and populated as per above rationality and [11159736: CSI-RS for Tracking in SSBURST slot](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktLX3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [11193236: patternId and ceAxCIndex choices](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvqrKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

PDCCH: DL full beam from downlinkBeamPatternId[0], no matter the PDCCH is for common channel or UE specific data transmission as per [11193236: patternId and ceAxCIndex choices](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvqrKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

PDSCH: 2 panel split beam for DL in downlinkBeamPatternId[1], downlinkBeamPatternId[2], no matter the PDSCH is for common channel or UE specific data transmission as per above rationality and [11139560: Precoding options](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_krn4q_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

CSI-RS for channel acquisition: 2 panel split beam for DL in downlinkBeamPatternId[1], downlinkBeamPatternId[2] and populated as per as per above rationality and [11193236: patternId and ceAxCIndex choices](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvqrKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

#### 2.4.3.10.0-2 FDD Beamforming L3 Call Beam ID for UL (ID: 12704269)

**2.4.3.10.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12704270`)

FDD Beamforming L3 Call If actBeamformingFdd (see [10967202: PsCell_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), the beam applied for UL channels/signals is as bellow according to [11113948: FDD mMIMO beamforming PatternID](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpfxufvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9):

PRACH: uplinkBeamPatternId[0] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and populated for 4RX as per above rationality and [11094230: FDD FR1 - subcell type and number of antennas](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaogfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

PUSCH: uplinkBeamPatternId[1~4] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for 8RX as per above rationality and [11112346: Apply Updated Beam ID to 8RX Physical Channels](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

PUCCH: uplinkBeamPatternId[1~4] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for 8RX as per above rationality and [11112346: Apply Updated Beam ID to 8RX Physical Channels](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

A-SRS: uplinkBeamPatternId[1~4] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) for 8RX as per above rationality and [11112346: Apply Updated Beam ID to 8RX Physical Channels](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

---

### 2.4.3.11 FDD Beamforming (ID: 12823079)
#### 2.4.3.11.0-1 FDD Beamforming Beam usage for DL (ID: 12823080)

**2.4.3.11.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `12823082`)

FDD Beamforming If FDD cell with actBeamforming = TRUE, the beam for all DL channel/signal shall be as bellow:

SSB: fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

CSI-RS for tracking: fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

PDCCH:fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)[CB008577-B] if dlMimoMode != CL_32x4MIMO, otherwise choose best PMI beam or fullSectorBeamPatternId[0], or best beam from PRACH probing for DL PDCCH [CB008577-C] and UL PDCCH[End CB008577-C] as per [12777257: FDD: Maximum Two beams from CSI feedback](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ldAtcI1KEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)[End CB008577-B];

PDSCH: as bellow table

                           [](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) [](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ldAtcI1KEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

| | | | | | | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| dlMimoMode | beam for common PDSCH | beam for PDSCH before CSI is not available | beam for PDSCH with CSI available | | beam for PDSCH when CSI aging | |
| [Before CB008577-B] not CL_32x4MIMO[End CB008577-B] | fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] and coppied for 4 stream | If actPrachProbing = FALSE, 2 identical beam from fullSectorBeamPatternId[0]; If actPrachProbing = TRUE, 2 idnetical DL beam mapping from PRACH probed best beam | beamset4PatternId[0~1] are selected from beamset#4 according to 11152417: SS/PBCH Index identification and association to an internal Beam ID in the gNB | | beamset4PatternId[0~1] are selected from beamset#4 according to 11152417: SS/PBCH Index identification and association to an internal Beam ID in the gNB | |
| [CB008577-B]CL_32x4MIMO [End CB008677-B] | one beam fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] for 2 stream | If actPrachProbing = FALSE, fullSectorBeamPatternId[0]; If actPrachProbing = TRUE, one DL beam mapping from PRACH probing best beam | initial TX：PMI beam from CSI report according to 12777257: FDD: Maximum Two beams from CSI feedback | reTx: if initial TX is on PMI beam, latest PMI beam from CSI report if latest reported rank = last reported rank; otherwise the same beam as initial TX if the initial beam is valid or fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] if the initial beam is invalid. | initial TX: one beam fullSectorBeamPatternId[0] for 2 stream | reTX: same beam as initial TX |

The mapping between DL beam and PRACH probing best beam is as the mapping between UL beam and Dl beam in beamset4PatternId as in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9).

CSI-RS for channel acquisition: beamset4PatternId[0~1] as per [11112298: Apply Updated Beam ID to Physical Channels](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpUymPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

#### 2.4.3.11.0-2 FDD Beamforming Beam usage for UL (ID: 12823081)

**2.4.3.11.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12823083`)

FDD Beamforming If FDD cell with actBeamforming = TRUE, the beam for all UL channel/signal shall be as bellow::

PRACH: if actPrachProbing = FALSE fullSectorBeamPatternId[1], otherwise beamset4PatternId[4~7] in [12679724: bfSetupFdd structure content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_WA-CYE_iEfCeeMlEb49MrQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

PUSCH: if actPrachProbing = TRUE, UL counterpart refine beam for best beam from PRACH detection and if actPrachProbing = FALSE, beamset4PatternId[4~7] [CB008577-C] without valid PMI beam from CSI, and after PMI beam from CSI , shall use split beam of UL PMI beam[End CB008577-C] as per [12771852: FDD: four beams for 8RX receiver](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__UhkgYlZEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9);

PUCCH: beamset4PatternId[4~7] as [12770624: FDD: Beams applicable to long PUCCH](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_JBaoA4iTEfCrqOdGJPoCRA?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

A-SRS: the same as PUSCH as per [11112346: Apply Updated Beam ID to 8RX Physical Channels](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpVZofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)

### 2.4.3.12 Test Support (ID: 11156287)
#### 2.4.3.12.0-1 Test Support patternId Offset for Test Usage (ID: 11156292)

**2.4.3.12.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156299`)

Test Support After 5G-L2-PS has determined the patternId according to 5G_UP_ALG_1939_replaced([11111782](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpQhIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), it shall add the value of parameter rdPatternIdOffset (5G_L2_1103_replaced([11183652](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27lMiPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) to the patternId before using it in message DlData_PatternConfigReq (5G_L1_5G21A_53). It is left for tester responsibility to set the parameter to a reasonable value.

---

## 2.4.4 DRX Control (ID: 11156305)

**2.4.4.0-1**  (ID: `11156309`)

DRX Control FA: [Internal Domain] DRX and Measurement Gap Control

Document Owner: Yao Wang (NSB); Yuanying Xie (NSB)

---

### 2.4.4.1 DRX Control for non-CA UEs (ID: 11156314)
#### 2.4.4.1.0-1 DRX Control for non-CA UEs 2.4.4.1.1 drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL (ID: 11156320)
##### 2.4.4.1.0-1.0-1 DRX Control for non-CA UEs 2.4.4.1.1.1 general of drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL (ID: 11156326)

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156330`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL is one per DL/UL HARQ process, maintained in L2PS DL/UL separately, the purpose is to keep a window within which the UE listens to the PDCCH for the corresponding HARQ. the detail value refer to 5G_L2_IF_426_replaced([10975509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBFLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. actDrxRetransTimerSupport

 This is the NIDD parameter for the CB009531 feature flag, for the different behavior with true or false of the actDrxRetransTimerSupport, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_15848_replaced([11114393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_2270_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_2271_replaced([11114484](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. start timer in L2PS DL/UL

 The trigger of the start of drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL in L2PS DL/UL, which is after the end symbol of drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_15848_replaced([11114393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_2270_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Define two types of drx-RetransmissionTimerUL

1) type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL: started by PUSCH CRC OK(ACK) with NIDD parameter actDrxRetransTimerSupport = true.

2) type2 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL: started by PUSCH CRC KO(NACK).

1. stop timer in L2PS DL/UL

 The drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL started by NACK should be stopped when the retransmission of corresponding HARQ is scheduled in L2PS DL/UL with high priority, so these kinds of timer exist quite short duration, which make it no necessary to exchange between L2PS DL/UL.

1. type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL

 When NIDD parameter actDrxRetransTimerSupport = true, the drx-RetransmissionTimerUL can be started by PUSCH CRC OK, which is different from DL that only started at the NACK of DL HARQ feedback.

L2PS chooses the harqId for new transmission from 0 - 15 sequencely per UE, the minimum interval of same harqId(do not consider retransmission) of the UE is about 80slots for TDD 1116 pattern and 16slots for FDD pattern. Based on that fact, L2PS starts/restarts type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL per UE, not per harq, which means there is no stop type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL proactively, except for the expiration of type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL.

---

##### 2.4.4.1.0-1.0-2 DRX Control for non-CA UEs 2.4.4.1.1.2 UE DRX status impacted by drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL (ID: 11156336)

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11156343`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs The drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL is introduced by 5GC000772 and 5GC002060, which say that "while UE DRX onDuration timer and inactivity timer are both off, the HARQ process can retransmit only when its DRX retransmission timer is running. ", so the UE DRX active state does not contain the drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL. We continue to keep this idea, and give some more chance to be scheduled in L2PS DL/UL when the drx-RetransmissionTimerUL is started by PUSCH CRC OK(type1 drx-RetransmissionTimerUL) with NIDD parameter actDrxRetransTimerSupport = true, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2270_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

Control for non-CA UEs 11156357: DRX Control for non-CA UEs There is timing delay for DL AN/UL CRC result received in L2PS. with drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL length of slot1/slot2, it can happen that the drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL has already expired before the DL AN/UL CRC result received in L2PS. If no other DRX timer( (drx-onDurationTimer and drx-InactivityTimer)) is running, then there is no chance for retransmission until next drx-onDurationTimer, which is as expected and accepted. Below picture shows an example, with drx-RetransmissionTimerDL length = 8slot. [image-0][image_desc]The image is a screenshot of a spreadsheet that appears to be tracking some kind of communication protocol, possibly related to cellular networks, given the terms "slot," "L2PS," "harq2," and "retx timer." The spreadsheet has two main rows labeled "slot in L2PS 1116 pattern K1" and "slot on air." Each row is divided into columns representing time slots, numbered from 0 upwards. The "slot in L2PS 1116 pattern K1" row shows a sequence of colored cells representing different types of slots: "D" (blue), "S" (green), and "U" (orange). Below these colored cells, there are numbers that seem to indicate durations or offsets. The "slot on air" row also shows a sequence of colored cells ("D," "S," "U") and corresponding numbers below them. Several annotations and arrows are present in the image: - An arrow points from a "D" slot in the "slot in L2PS" row at slot 7 to a cell labeled "harq2." - Another arrow points from the "harq2" label to a "D" slot in the "slot on air" row at slot 7. - A green rectangle is drawn around a cell in the "slot in L2PS" row between slots 9 and 10. - A text box labeled "pucch received for harq2: nack" is positioned above the "slot on air" row, pointing to slot 14. - A text box labeled "start retx timer harq2" is positioned above the "slot in L2PS" row, pointing to slot 18. - A text box labeled "stop retx timer harq2" is positioned above the "slot in L2PS" row, pointing to slot 2. - A yellow rectangle labeled "retx timer DL hard2" spans from slot 19 to slot 2 in the "slot in L2PS" row. - A yellow rectangle labeled "RTT time" spans from slot 14 to slot 15 in the "slot on air" row. - An arrow points from slot 18 in the "slot in L2PS" row to the "stop retx timer harq2" text box. - An arrow points from slot 2 in the "slot in L2PS" row to a "harq2" label. - An arrow points from the "harq2" label to slot 2 in the "slot on air" row. - A yellow rectangle labeled "retx timer DL harq2" spans from slot 18 to slot 3 in the "slot on air" row. ##### 2.4.4.1.0-1.0-4 DRX Control for non-CA UEs 2.4.4.1.1.4 enable drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL (ID: 11156363)

**2.4.4.1.0-1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11156367`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs L2PS starts UE DRX activation procedure when receiving PsUser_UserModifyReq with drxConfig structure is present and IE 'drxConfig' of drxConfig structure is set to 1, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2263_replaced([11114367](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Actually when UE receives RRC reconfiguration msg for drx configuration, UE may change to DRX deactive immediately, which leads to DRX status mismatch between gNB and UE. It may be worse with the introduction of drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL, so it’s decided to start drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL after receiving PsUser_RrcReconfCompletedReq in L2PS, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2270_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

DrxConfigurationMode with value of activated is involved in the PsUser_RrcReconfCompletedReq msg, refer to 5G_L2_IF_2575_replaced([10977732](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VOghfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

11156385: DRX Control for non-CA UEs 11156385: DRX Control for non-CA UEs 11156385: DRX Control for non-CA UEs 11156385: DRX Control for non-CA UEs 11156385: DRX Control for non-CA UEs 11156385: DRX Control for non-CA UEs 11156385: DRX Control for non-CA UEs drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL is one per DL/UL HARQ process, the purpose is to determine the start symbol of the drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2271_replaced([11114484](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). As currently the slot based scheduling is supported in L2PS, for the simplicity, the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL is multiple of 14 symbols, which values are introduced by new NIDD parameter drxProfile1/drxHarqRttTimerDl, drxProfile2/drxHarqRttTimerDl, drxProfileNrAnr/drxHarqRttTimerDl, drxProfile1/drxHarqRttTimerUl, drxProfile2/drxHarqRttTimerUl, drxProfileNrAnr/drxHarqRttTimerUl, and sent to L2PS by PsUser_UserSetupReq/PsUser_UserModifyReq, refer to 5G_L2_IF_426_replaced([10975509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBFLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)). 1. start timer The start of drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL would be in the beginning of the slot boundary. Actually there is timing delay for DL L2PS receiving the DL AN on PUCCH/PUSCH, and also timing delay for UL L2PS receiving PUSCH CRC result from L1. The timing delay is caused by scheduling advance in L2PS and receiving delay for L1 processing time. For scheduling advance, refer to 5G_L2_455_replaced([11152781](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24rCcvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Considering the timing delay in FDD, TDD FR1, TDD FR2, it's decided that the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL is restricted with max value of 42 symbols, which is no larger than the timing delay, so there is no need to start the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL. These are the following benefits with this handling: 1) the retransmission could be scheduled without delay caused by the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL. 2) the implementation would be more simple in L2PS without the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL/drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerUL. Below picture shows an example of the timing delay for TDD 1116 pattern, there is 4 slot delay that the DL AN on PUCCH for slot14 received in slot18 in DL L2PS. So with this delay, the drx-HARQ-RTT-TimerDL has already expired when DL AN on PUCCH received in DL L2PS. [image-0][image_desc]The image is a screenshot of a spreadsheet that appears to be tracking some kind of data transmission or communication protocol. It is divided into two main sections, labeled "slot in L2PS 1116 pattern K1" and "slot on air". Each section is further divided into columns representing time slots, numbered from 0 to 18. Within these time slots, there are colored cells containing letters: 'D' (likely for Downlink), 'U' (likely for Uplink), and 'S' (possibly for Special or Synchronization). Below the "slot in L2PS" section, there are rows of numbers that seem to correspond to the time slots above them. There are also annotations on the image: - An arrow points from slot 7 in the "slot in L2PS" section to slot 7 in the "slot on air" section, with the label "harq2". - An arrow points from slot 18 in the "slot in L2PS" section to a point above it, with the label "start retx timer harq2". - An arrow points from slot 14 in the "slot on air" section to a point above it, with the label "pucch received for harq2: nack". - A yellow highlighted cell in the "slot on air" section, spanning slots 14 and 15, is labeled "RTT time". #### 2.4.4.1.0-3 DRX Control for non-CA UEs 2.4.4.1.3 drx-InactivityTimer (ID: 11156391)
##### 2.4.4.1.0-3.0-1 DRX Control for non-CA UEs 2.4.4.1.3.1 general of drx-InactivityTimer (ID: 11156394)

**2.4.4.1.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156400`)

DRX Control for non-CA UEs drx-InactivityTimer is one per UE, the purpose is to keep a window within which UE listens to the PDCCH. L2PS should start/restart drx-InactivityTimer when new transmission DL or UL grant is scheduled, the value is in unit of ms, refer to 5G_L2_IF_426_replaced([10975509](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VBFLvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The drx-InactivityTimer is maintained in DL/UL L2PS separately, and sync the drx-InactivityTimer status to each other when the status is changed, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2267_replaced([11114433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

As UE start drx-InactivityTimer after the PDCCH symbol, then the timer would be stopped at the same symbol of the last drx-InactivityTimer subframe at UE side, but L2PS stops the drx-InactivityTimer at the latter part of the last subframe, which means L2PS stop the drx-InactivityTimer later than the UE side. So if in the last subframe of drx-InactivityTimer window, L2PS gives grant in the PDCCH symbol index that larger than the PDCCH symbol index where UE starts the drx-InactivityTimer, then UE will miss this grant, DTX happen.

For the drx-InactivityTimer back off mechanism, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2268_replaced([11114436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.4.4.2 DRX Control for CA UEs (ID: 11156406)
#### 2.4.4.2.0-1 DRX Control for CA UEs 2.4.4.2.1 drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL (ID: 11156409)
##### 2.4.4.2.0-1.0-1 DRX Control for CA UEs 2.4.4.2.1.1 general of drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL (ID: 11156414)

**2.4.4.2.0-1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11156419`)

DRX Control for CA UEs Each Scell maintains its own drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL separately, no need to sync to other Scells and Pcell.

1. actDrxRetransTimerSupport

 The actDrxRetransTimerSupport shall be sent to Scell with PsUser_UserSetupReq message. For the different behavior with true or false of the actDrxRetransTimerSupport in Scell, which is the same as Pcell, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_15848_replaced([11114393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_2270_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_2271_replaced([11114484](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1. start and stop timer in each Scell

 For the start and stop of drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL in each scell(including DL and UL L2PS), which is the same as Pcell, refer to 5G_L2_14575_replaced([11156326](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24-kevvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.4.4.2.0-1.0-2 DRX Control for CA UEs 2.4.4.2.1.2 enable drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL (ID: 11156425)

**2.4.4.2.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11156430`)

DRX Control for CA UEs Same as 5G_L2_14579_replaced([11156363](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_LgPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), Scell drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL shall be started after receiving PsUser_RrcReconfCompletedReq in L2PS with the drxConfigurationMode = activated for DRX setup, inlcuding inter-gNB CA Scell, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_2270_replaced([11114455](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: For DRX setup is done before Scell addition, DRX config will be sent to Scell with PsUser_UserSetupReq in Scell addition procedure, while no DRX config sent to Pcell with PsUser_UserModifyReq. For this case, drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL already started in Pcell, then Scell drx-RetransmissionTimerDL/drx-RetransmissionTimerUL could be started after PsUser_UserSetupReq, no PsUser_RrcReconfCompletedReq with the drxConfigurationMode = activated would be received in Scell L2PS.

### 2.4.4.3 DRX Control (InactivityTimer) for Multi L2-RT CA UEs (ID: 11156434)
#### 2.4.4.3.1 DRX Overview for FR1 CA (ID: 11156439)
##### 2.4.4.3.1.0-1 DRX Overview for FR1 CA Common DRX State management (ID: 11156444)

**2.4.4.3.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12798802`)

DRX Overview for FR1 CA For FR1 CA,

Each Scell also has UE DRX configuration and maintain DRX lifecycle as 5G_UP_ALG_2262_replaced([11114321](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDJPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

Each SCell also shall maintain the inactivity timer status by exchanging its downlink and uplink(if Scell is DL+UL joint Scell) inactivity timer status, as 5G_UP_4751_replaced([11356011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucVfl_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_2274_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

To manage DRX status per serving cell, below DRX states is proposed:

Common Status for both Pcell and Scell**:**

OnDuration timer status: it is UE level configuration, each CC has same onDuration time pattern as 5G_UP_ALG_2262_replaced([11114321](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDJPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Local DRX InactivityTimer status: Each CC has both local DL and UL(if Scell is configed with DL+UL).

Pcell Local DRX inactivityTimer status management is defined in 5G_L2_8371_replaced([11156458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ylPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

Scell is defined by 5G_L2_8380_replaced([11156603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZq_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

Local DRX InactivityTimer should be synced between DL and UL as 5G_UP_ALG_2267_replaced([11114433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

Besides Local DRX InactivityTimer Status, each CC also need known remote cell's DRX inactivityTimer status in order to sync with UE final DRX status, the reason is the UE inactivityTimer will be extended as 5G_UP_ALG_2267_replaced([11114433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) once latest grant is received from any serving cell.

Remote DRX InactivityTimer states in Pcell:

*RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i]:*

remote Scell i local DRX InactivityTimer status; By default is OFF when SCELL activated, updated based on Reporting from Scell once state change as 5G_L2_8381_replaced([11156653](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25BAsvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

*InactivityTimerPsPeer (in Pcell): combined all Scell's *RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[] as per 5G_L2_8421_replaced([11156499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ymfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

Remote DRX InactivityTimer states in Scell:

*InactivityTimerPsPeer (in Scell):*

By default is OFF. Update based on received broadcast message from Pcell for UE level overall DRX inactivityTimer state change in 5G_L2_8418_replaced([11156623](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZrvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Based on Local Drx inactivityTimer status and remote DRX inactivityTimer status, UE level final DRX inactivityTimer status is determined in Pcell and synced to Scells:**

UE level Final DrxInactivityTimerState in Pcell:

Pcell DL as DRX master and make decision of UE level overall DRX inactivityTimer status according to 5G_L2_8422_replaced([11156522](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ynvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Pcell **broadcast it to all serving Scells DL and UL at same time as 5G_L2_8372_replaced([11156550](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZpPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), once CHANGED(ON to OFF or OFF to ON)**

UE level Final DrxInactivityTimerState in Scell:

Scell decide status based on 5G_L2_8419_replaced([11156638](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZsfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , combined Scell local DRX inactivityTimer status and *InactivityTimerPsPeer (in Scell).*

Note that if *InactivityTimerPsPeer is changed from **ON to OFF, local DRX inactivityTimer state will force to OFF. As 5G_UP_ALG_2277_replaced([11114541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), all scell stop scheduling once Pcell broadcast Final *DrxInactivityTimerState is OFF, to avoid mismatch/out of sync between Pcell and Scells due to DRX sync message delay.****

For SR triggered DRX inactivityTimer in Pcell, Pcell will broadcast Final *DrxInactivityTimerState to scell once SR is verified as 5G_UP_ALG_2281_replaced([11114555](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpl4UfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

Notes:

This requirement is the overall description based on latest DRX sync framework. For new introduced functions, refer to CFAM content of related feature. **[Before CNI-137616-B]This DRX sync framework is controlled by R&D parameter rdEnable6137Fr1CaDrxSolution defined as 5G_L2_10442_replaced([11184728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27tIWfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**[End CNI-137616-B]****

11156494: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156494: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156494: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156494: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156494: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156494: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156494: P(S)Cell DRX handling For FR1 CA, each serving cell shall maintain its local DRX inactivityTimer. Each serving cell only use its own grant and HARQ feedback information to update the local DRX inactivityTimer state. Details refer to 5G_UP_ALG_15848_replaced([11114393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_2267_replaced([11114433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_2274_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

PCell shall maintain a pair of timestamp in XSFN format [lastOffXsfn, lastOnXsfn] to record latest local DRX inactivityTimer state change time XSFNchange as follows:

if state change from ON to OFF:

update lastOffXsfn value to the state change time XSFNchange , update lastOnXsfn value to INVALID value.

else if state change from OFF to ON:

update lastOnXsfn value to the state change time XSFNchange .

else:

no action.

The INVALID value is the largest value that the underlaying type of XSFN can represent.

The value of XSFNchange should aligned to TDD FR1 30kHz subcarrier spacing SCStddFr1 according to PCell's local time XSFNpcell and subcarrier spacing SCSpcell as:

XSFNchange = XSFNpcell * SCStddFr1 / SCSpcell

If DRX inactivityTimer reset indication triggered by PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) refer to 5G_L2_8422_replaced([11156522](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ynvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), PCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) may sync new DRX state to DL by message PsInternal_UlToDlIntraSchedUpdate due to UL inactivityTimer state change caused by new UL grant. In order to avoid furher mismatch between PCell DL and UL and also avoid further mismatch between PCell and SCells, PCell DL shall ignore this DRX sync message crossed with DRX inactivityTimer reset indication.

Could not load image (check sign-in and GCS access).

[PR767017] The keyword XSFN used above is just an expression way of timestamp. DEV team could select proper data type to implement it. For example, use XHFN instead as XSFN type can only be compared within 5.12s time range. [End PR767017]

---

##### 2.4.4.3.2.0-2 P(S)Cell DRX handling Pcell remote DRX Inactivity Timer Status Management (ID: 11156499)

**2.4.4.3.2.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11156515`)

P(S)Cell DRX handling PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall maintain remote DRX inactivityTimer state RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] for each remote SCells.

i: remote cell config index in PCell 5G-L2-PS.

RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] is updated by following event:

- PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) received message PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivitityTimerStatusInd refer to 5G_L2_IF_2774_replaced([10980770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VlF2Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from corresponding remote SCell.

If SCell release refer to 5G_L2_2872_replaced([11160006](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25UiufvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) triggered for remote SCell i , UE context on remote SCell may be removed during DRX inactivity timer state ON, so no more PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivitityTimerStatusInd could be received for such SCell to set RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] to OFF. In order to handle this case, PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall set RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] to OFF after SCell release process finished.

PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall maintain one remote DRX inactivityTimer overall state InactivityTimerPsPeer for all remote SCells. When any value of RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] is ON, InactivityTimerPsPeer is ON. Otherwise, InactivityTimerPsPeer is OFF. InactivityTimerPsPeer is updated by following event:

- RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] value is changed.

PCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) shall maintain one remote DRX inactivityTimer overall state InactivityTimerPsPeer for all remote SCells. InactivityTimerPsPeer in PCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) is aligned with same sate in PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) by DL to UL intra update message PsInternal_DlToUlIntraSchedUpdate once InactivityTimerPsPeer value in PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) is changed.

PCell shall maintain a pair of timestamp in xSFN format [lastOffXsfn, lastOnXsfn] for each remote serving cells to record remote serving cells latest DRX inactivityTimer state change timing indicated by message PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStautsInd refer to 5G_L2_IF_2774_replaced([10980770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VlF2Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) as follows:

For each state report received in PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStautsInd:

if state change from ON to OFF:

update lastOffXsfn value to the state change time XSFNchange, update lastOnXsfn value to INVALID value.

else if state change from OFF to ON:

update lastOnXsfn value to the state change time XSFNchange.

else:

no action.

The INVALID value is the largest value that the underlaying type of XSFN can represent.

The value of XSFNchange shall align to TDD FR1 30kHz subcarrier SCStddFr1 according to SCell's local time XSFNscell got from message PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStautsInd and subcarrier spacing SCSscell as:

XSFNchange = XSFNscell * SCStddFr1 / SCSscell

[PR767017] The keyword XSFN used above is just an expression way of timestamp. DEV team could select proper data type to implement it. For example, use XHFN instead as XSFN type can only be compared within 5.12s time range. [End PR767017]

---

11156543: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156543: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156543: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156543: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156543: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156543: P(S)Cell DRX handling 11156543: P(S)Cell DRX handling Whenever PCell local DRX inactivityTimer state changed or inactivityTimerPsPeer changed, PCell shall decide final DRX inactivityTimer state DrxInactivityTimerState on PCell. DrxInactivityTimerState is also GNB decided UE level final DRX inactivityTimer state. DrxInactivityTimerState on PCell is ON if either PCell local DRX inactivityTimer state or InactivityTimerPsPeer is ON. Otherwise, DrxInactivityTimerState is OFF. As specified in requirement 5G_UP_ALG_2277_replaced([11114541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the conditions for DrxInactivityTimerState turn to OFF should take into account DRX inactivityTimer sync delays between SCell and PCell. PCell shall maintain DRX inactivityTimer state change history for each serving cells as specified in 5G_L2_8371_replaced([11156458](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ylPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_L2_8421_replaced([11156499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ymfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) so that PCell could decide DrxInactivityTimerState according to DRX inactivityTimer state on each serving cells on the same slot. After PCell updated DRX inactivityTimer state change time XSFNchange for any serving cells as specified in 5G_L2_8421_replaced([11156499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ymfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), PCell shall search all [lastOffXsfn, lastOnXsfn] pairs to find if any overlapping slot exist among all serving cells. If so, DrxInactivityTimerState change to OFF. Example refer to below diagram: [xlsx_desc]--- Sheet1 --- When PCELL receive latest DRX inactivityTimer state 0 (OFF) from SCELL, PCELL's state may already changed to 1 (ON) due to newTx. NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN Sync delay example 1: NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN XSFN 0 1 2.0 3.0 4 5 6 7.0 8.0 9.0 PCELL 1 1 1.0 1.0 0 0 1 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStateInd NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN SCELL 1 1 1.0 1.0 0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN Sync delay example 2: NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN XSFN 0 1 2.0 3.0 4 5 6 7.0 8.0 9.0 PCELL 1 1 1.0 1.0 1 0 1 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStateInd NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN SCELL 1 1 1.0 1.0 0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN PCELL maintain XSFN pair [lastOffXsfn, lastOnXsfn] for each serving cell represent latest XSFN range in which DRX inativityTimer state is 0(OFF). NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN XSFN pair update example: NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN XSFN 0 1 2.0 3.0 4 5 6 7.0 8.0 9.0 PCELL 0 1 1.0 1.0 0 0 1 NaN NaN NaN [lastOffXsfn, lastOnXsfn] [0, X] [0, 1] NaN NaN [4, X] NaN [4, 6] NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN SCELL 0 1 1.0 1.0 0 NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN [lastOffXsfn, lastOnXsfn] [0, X] [0, 1] NaN NaN [4, X] NaN NaN NaN NaN NaN[/xlsx_desc][xlsx_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/attachments/m24dng_5G_L2_Entity_Level_file2388_0.xlsx[/xlsx_path] File: FR1_CA_DRX_sync_delay.xslx Folder: After PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) decide DrxInactivityTimerState change to OFF caused by remote SCell sync message, besides sync the state change to all SCells as specified in 5G_L2_8372_replaced([11156550](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZpPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall stop local ongoing inactivityTimer if exist, reset all DRX inactivityTimer state and sync a DRX inactivityTimer reset request to PCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) by DL to UL intra update message. PCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) shall stop local ongoing inactivityTimer if exist and reset all DRX inactivityTimer state after receive request from PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL). [PR767017] Once PCELL decide UE level final DRX inactivityTimer to OFF, in addition to sync the state to each serving cell. PCELL shall reset local maintained DRX inactivityTimer related state for each serving cells. - RemoteCellDrxInactivityTimerState[i] state reset to 0. - lastOnXsfn reset to invalid value and lastOffXsfn reset to current on air XSFN. The keyword XSFN used above is just an expression way of timestamp. DEV team could select proper data type to implement it. For example, use XHFN instead as XSFN type can only be compared within 5.12s time range. [End PR767017]

##### 2.4.4.3.2.0-4 P(S)Cell DRX handling Pcell Sync UE Level Final DRX Inactivity Timer Status to Scell (ID: 11156550)

**2.4.4.3.2.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11156559`)

P(S)Cell DRX handling [Before CB006137-AC]

To synchronize status of DRX Inactivity Timer between the 5G-L2-PS instances, PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) sends the InactivityTimer status to SCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) in the PsPeerCtrl_PCellStatusInd.

The DRX InactivityTimer status is ON if either PCell UL InactivityTimer or/and PCell DL inactivityTimer is running.

PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) sends its DRX InactivityTimer status:

- on reception of harq feedback for scell.

- or when it changes to OFF

The InactivityTimer status can change to OFF on one of following events:

- PCell (DL) receives a notification from UL scheduler that UL timer status is OFF

- DL Inactivity Timer expires

- Rollback happens when DTX as described in 5G_UP_ALG_2274_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB006137-AC]

To synchronize status of DRX inactivityTimer between 5G-L2-PS instances, PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall broadcast DrxInactivityTimerState to SCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) and SCell 5G-L2-PS (UL) by message PsPeerCtrl_PCellStatusInd when state changed.

The DrxInactivityTimerState can change to ON/OFF on one of following event:

- PCell (DL) receives a notification from UL scheduler that UL timer status is ON/OFF

- DL Inactivity Timer start or expire

- Rollback happens when DTX as described in 5G_UP_ALG_2274_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

- PCell (DL) received new remote SCell state by message PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivitityTimerStatusInd cause InactivityTimerPsPeer value changed as specified in 5G_L2_8421_replaced([11156499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24_ymfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB006137-AC]

11156592: P(S)Cell DRX handling [Before CB006137-AC]

When P(S)Cell InactivityTimer status changes to OFF in PCell 5G-L2-PS, PCell L2PS notifies SCell L2PS via the message PsPeerCtrl_PCellStatusInd. However before receiving the notification from PCell, SCell 5G-L2-PS may grant the UE (please refer to 5G_L1L2_FLOW_479_replaced([11197286](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Bf5nO_vREe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more information on slot timing).

To avoid that PCell 5G-L2-PS changes the DRX state of the UE (and start traffic) based on a wrong grant on SCell, PCell 5G-L2-PS shall not start InactivityTimer on the reception of ScellUeSchedulingInd if its InactivityTimer status is OFF (UL and DL InactivityTimer are not running) and the SCell grant(s) were sent on DRX OFF period (according to the DRX cycle).

Could not load image (check sign-in and GCS access).

Could not load image (check sign-in and GCS access).

[CB006137-AC]

When PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) decide UE level final DRX inactivityTimer state DrxInactivityTimerState changes to OFF, PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) notifies SCell L2PS via the message PsPeerCtrl_PCellStatusInd. However before receiving the OFF notification from PCell, SCell 5G-L2-PS DL may sync new DRX state to PCell via message PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStatusInd refer to 5G_L2_IF_2774_replaced([10980770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VlF2Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) due to SCell local inactivityTimer state change caused by new DL/UL grant on SCell. In order to avoid furher mismatch between PCell and SCells due to DRX sync message delay as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_2277_replaced([11114541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), PCell shall ignore DRX sync message from SCell if SCell indicated InactivityTimerPsPeer on SCell as specified in 5G_L2_IF_2774_replaced([10980770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VlF2Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is ON.

Could not load image (check sign-in and GCS access).

[End CB006137-AC]

handling 11156618: SCell DRX handling 11156618: SCell DRX handling 11156618: SCell DRX handling [Before CB006137-AC] 5G-L2-PS on SCell maintains an inactivityTimer per UE as described in 5G_UP_ALG_2268_replaced([11114436](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) considering only its grants.

[CB006137-AC]

For FR1 CA, each serving cell shall maintain its local DRX inactivityTimer. Each serving cell only use its own grant and HARQ feedback information to update the local DRX inactivityTimer state. Details refer to 5G_UP_ALG_15848_replaced([11114393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkDL_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , 5G_UP_ALG_2267_replaced([11114433](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpkqNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_2274_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If SCell received UE level final DRX inactivityTimer state by message PsPeerCtrl_PcellStatusInd as specified in 5G_L2_8372_replaced([11156550](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZpPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is OFF, SCell shall overwrite local DRX inactivityTimer to OFF in order to stop DL/UL scheduling on SCell as specified in 5G_UP_ALG_2277_replaced([11114541](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

However before SCell 5G-L2-PS UL receiving the OFF notification from PCell, SCell 5G-L2-PS UL may sync new DRX state to PCell via message PsInternal_UlToDlIntraSchedUpdate due to SCell UL inactivityTimer state change caused by new UL grant on SCell. In order to avoid furher mismatch between SCell DL and UL and also avoid further mismatch between PCell and SCells, SCell DL shall ignore DRX sync message from SCell UL crossed with OFF notification from PCell.

Could not load image (check sign-in and GCS access).

[End CB006137-AC].

##### 2.4.4.3.3.0-2 SCell DRX handling Scell remote DRX Inactivity Timer Status Management (ID: 11156623)

**2.4.4.3.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11156633`)

SCell DRX handling [Before CB006137-AC]

On reception of PsPeer_PCellStatusInd(5G_L2_IF_689_replaced([10979939](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Ve_Mfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) message, the SCell 5G-L2-PS scheduler should for each UE within the message:

- If a configured UE is indicated as isInactivityTimerOn OFF in the drxStateList entry corresponding to its RNTI then local inactivityTimer (5G_L2_8380_replaced([11156603](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZq_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is stopped if running .

- If a configured UE is indicated as isInactivityTimerOn ON in the drxStateList corresponding to its RNTI then the UE SCellDRXState is set to ON

[CB006137-AC]

SCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) and 5G-L2-PS (UL) (if not DL only SCell) shall maintain one remote DRX inactivityTimer state InactivityTimerPsPeer separately. InactivityTimerPsPeer value on SCell is updated according to PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) broadcast UE level final DRX inactivityTimer state by message PsPeerCtrl_PcellStatusInd as specified in 5G_L2_8372_replaced([11156550](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_25AZpPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

On reception of PsPeer_PCellStatusInd(5G_L2_IF_689_replaced([10979939](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2Ve_Mfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) message, the SCell 5G-L2-PS scheduler should for each UE within the message:

- update InactivityTimerPsPeer value to latest value within message.

[End CB006137-AC]

---

##### 2.4.4.3.3.0-3 SCell DRX handling Scell Decide UE Level Final DRX Inactivity Timer Status (ID: 11156638)

**2.4.4.3.3.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11156648`)

SCell DRX handling [Before CB006137-AC]

5G-L2-PS on SCell maintains an UE specific state SCellDRXState.

SCellDRXState is ON to indicates that the UE is ready to decode PDDCH/PDSCH transmission on SCell (i.e. ACTIVE due to ON phasis of the cycle or recent activity), and SCellDRXState is OFF to indicates that the UE is not ready due to RX inactivity.

When ScellDRXState is set to OFF the UE is not anymore considered as eligible for scheduling and no more PDCCH/PDSCH are sent by SCell 5G-L2-PS.

In FR1 CA UE, the SCellDrxState is ON if the PCell InactivityTimer status reported in PsPeer_PCellStatusInd (isInactivityTimerOn) is ON.

[CB006137-AC]

Final DRX inactivityTimer state DrxInactivityTimerState on SCell should take into account SCell local DRX inactivityTimer state and InactivityTimerPsPeer on SCell.

DrxInactivityTimerState on SCell is ON if either SCell local DRX inactivityTimer state or InactivityTimerPsPeer is ON. Otherwise, DrxInactivityTimerState on SCell is OFF.

[End CB006137-AC]

---

##### 2.4.4.3.3.0-4 SCell DRX handling Scell Sync Local DRX Inactivity Timer Status to Pcell (ID: 11156653)

**2.4.4.3.3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11156663`)

SCell DRX handling To synchronize status of DRX inactivityTimer between 5G-L2-PS instances, SCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) shall send local DRX inactivityTimer state change to PCell 5G-L2-PS (DL) by message PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStautsInd refer to 5G_L2_IF_2774_replaced([10980770](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VlF2Pu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The local DRX inactivityTimer state change on SCell only take into account inactivityTimer state on local DL and local UL (if not DL only SCell) scheduler. Local state can change to ON/OFF on one of following event:

- SCell (DL) receives a notification from UL scheduler that UL timer status is ON/OFF

- DL Inactivity Timer start or expire

- Rollback happens when DTX as described in 5G_UP_ALG_2274_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB006137-C-UPD003]

SCell (DL) collect local DRX inactivityTimer state change info to message PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStautsInd from the begining of each slot and send message to PCell at the begining of next slot. In order to reduce state sync delay from SCell to PCell, SCell (DL) could use PsInternal_UlToDlIntraSchedUpdate message as a extra trigger to send message PsPeerCtrl_ScellDrxInactivityTimerStautsInd to PCell. After the message send process triggered by UL to DL intra update message, SCell (DL) still need to start a new local DRX inactivityTimer state collection process and send message to PCell at the begining of next slot because local DRX inactivityTimer state could be changed due to HARQ DTX message received after UL to DL intra update message.

[End CB006137-C-UPD003]

---

##### 2.4.4.3.3.0-5 SCell DRX handling Scell Proved Grant Update (ID: 11156668)

**2.4.4.3.3.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11156673`)

SCell DRX handling On reception of PsPeer_ScellPuXchReceiveIndHarqD(5G_L2_IF_670_replaced([10979692](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2VdKA_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), SCell 5G-L2-PS shall update prove grant according to example of 5G_UP_ALG_2274_replaced([11114532](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kplRS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[PR618166]

As 2271-B enabled UEs do not send padding bytes in UL direction for AUG grants and can trigger retransmission of grants, L2-PS shall skip issuing AUG UL grants for 2271-B enabled UEs when there is only RLC Status PDU transmission on the DL SRB

[END PR618166]
